programme.desc: A man is suspected of violence toward three cats, and a dog whose vision was being restricted by its matted fur receives help

programme.title: Animal Cops Philadelphia

Documentary following the work of the SPCAs Humane Law Enforcement team, which investigates cases of animal cruelty

programme.title: Dr Jeff: Rocky Mountain Vet
programme.desc: Dr Jeff and the team are on a time limit to treat an English bulldog who swallowed a rock. Hector gets a workout from some puppies in need

programme.title: Pit Bulls and Parolees
programme.desc: Tia races to rescue four dogs at a rural shelter in Louisiana from euthanasia. Mariah and Toney give a scared, shy dog a shot at a forever home

programme.title: Pit Bulls and Parolees
programme.desc: The team are faced with an emotional and physical challenge in the form of a severely under-nourished pit bull. They also have to rehabilitate a dog left in a shop

programme.title: The Zoo: San Diego
programme.desc: The youngest elephant at the park learns how to use its trunk, while an Andean bear is ready to start mating. Jeff searches for the elusive coast patch-nosed snake

programme.title: The Zoo: San Diego
programme.desc: A white rhino is pregnant with a very special calf. Plus, Connor the bone-loving Malayan tiger has tooth trouble and the vets step in to help

programme.title: Treehouse Masters
programme.desc: Pete Nelson creates an off-the-grid escape that is all about peace, love, and treehouses. Once built, the owner adds a massive hammock in the trees

programme.title: Preposterous Pets
programme.desc: The story of an obsessive fan of lizards, who changed his name to Henry Lizardlover and found fame by taking photos of his scaly friends posing as humans

programme.title: How Do Animals Do That?
programme.desc: How does a fish make a weapon out of water? Why do turtles always return to the same beach to lay their eggs? Why do rhinos all poop in the same place?

programme.title: How Do Animals Do That?
programme.desc: Experts spill the secrets of the means of communication used by cats. Plus, some clever monkeys who have turned to a life of crime

programme.title: Lair Of The Sawfish
programme.desc: Experts unravel the mystery behind one of the oceans strangest creatures, the sawfish, from its evolution to its role in the marine ecosystem

programme.title: Alaska: Surviving the Last Frontier
programme.desc: Following four groups of ordinary people as they travel into the wilderness of Alaska, where they must find a new home in the inhospitable environment and deal with the threat posed by bears, glaciers and their own fears

programme.title: Must Love Cats

programme.title: Lone Star Law

programme.title: Dr Jeff: Rocky Mountain Vet
programme.desc: Dr Jeff tries to mend a German shepherds broken elbow for its war veteran owner, and a cat with a swollen bladder needs difficult surgery to save its life

programme.title: Dr Jeff: Rocky Mountain Vet
programme.desc: Dr Jeff tries to save a dog kicked in the head by a horse, and tackles a neighbourhood overrun with feral cats. A pet ferret displays worrying symptoms

programme.title: Pit Bulls and Parolees
programme.desc: Before heading to New York Citys Dog Bowl, Mariah and M2 prepare their most eligible rescues for the big day. Tony and Earl discover a wounded stray

programme.title: Pit Bulls and Parolees
programme.desc: Moe and Lizzy reveal they are expecting a child and the family band together to host a gender reveal party. Lizzy goes the extra mile for her favourite dog

programme.title: The Zoo: San Diego
programme.desc: A baby hippo arrives and an orphaned koala needs support. A team travel to Kenya to share their innovative milk formula for elephant calves

programme.title: Treehouse Masters
programme.desc: Pete Nelson sets out to create a treehouse bed-and-breakfast designed for romance, featuring stained glass, a bedroom and the biggest bathroom Pete has ever built

programme.title: Meet the Sloths
programme.desc: Two rehabilitated sloths are released back into the wild, the team reveals the athletic secrets of the jungle gym, and the resident scientist inspects a sloth skeleton

programme.title: Meet the Sloths
programme.desc: A tracking system is tested by sanctuary favourite Sidwiggy, the nursery manager becomes an anger management counsellor, and the scientist discusses sloth fur

programme.title: How Do Animals Do That?
programme.desc: More secrets from the animal kingdom, including why moths are attracted to flames, why turtles cry and why sea otters bond with stones

programme.title: How Do Animals Do That?
programme.desc: Experts spill the secrets of the animal kingdom, revealing why spiders dont get stuck in their own webs and how foxes find water in the desert

programme.sub-title: In the Heat of the Moment

programme.desc: Dr Ps kennels reach maximum capacity, and he gives James Giuliani three days to move all his rescues out. The team vow to respond by finding a place of their own

programme.title: Alaska Gold Diggers
programme.desc: SJ and her daughters pool their savings and head to Nome, Alaska, in an effort to reopen the family gold mines and keep their legacy alive

programme.title: Tanked
programme.desc: Six of the companys employees - Brett, Wayde, Heather, Agnes, Redneck and the General - take a trip down memory lane by each choosing their favourite tanks built by ATM

programme.title: Alaska: Homestead Rescue
programme.desc: The Raneys visit a homestead in Oregon where fire is a constant threat and the hungry family see their livestock more like family pets than food

programme.title: Great Hammerhead Invasion
programme.desc: Leading shark scientist Tristan Guttridge tries to find out why, every November, the usually solitary giant hammerhead sharks congregate off the coast of Florida

programme.title: Super Predator
programme.desc: Film-maker Dave Riggs embarks on a quest to determine what creature managed to savagely devour a nine-foot-long great white shark by donning a one-man underwater exosuit and venturing over 1,000 feet to the bottom of Bremers Canyon, off Western Australia

programme.desc: Part one of two. Every 50 years villagers in Bangladesh must prepare for a surge of black rats. Rodent expert Dr Steven Belmain attempts to solve the mystery

programme.title: My Cat from Hell
programme.desc: Marco and Aimee recently got married, but Marcos cat Monty is tearing them apart with his appetite for destruction - and will eat everything in sight

programme.title: Preposterous Pets
programme.desc: The story of an obsessive fan of lizards, who changed his name to Henry Lizardlover and found fame by taking photos of his scaly friends posing as humans

programme.title: Tanked
programme.desc: Wayde and Brett try to come up with a solution to stop the acrylic from scratching off a skateboard ramp tank. Meanwhile, the ATM team builds an octopus tank for Waydes birthday

programme.title: Timewatch
programme.desc: A profile of the 20th-century escapologist, featuring restored archive footage and interviews with historians and illusionists, who recall his impact on audiences. Plus, revealing some of the secrets behind some of Houdinis most famous escapes. Originally broadcast in 2000

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: Gary Davies presents the pop chart programme, first broadcast on April 23, 1987. Featuring Five Star, The Smiths, Kim Wilde and Junior, Judy Boucher, Starship, Tom Jones, Glenn and Chris, Madonna and Terence Trent DArby

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: Jackie Brambles hosts the edition first aired on April 19, with performances by artists including Sonia, the Quireboys, Adamski, the Blues Brothers, Alannah Myles, the Adventures of Stevie V, Faith No More, the Family Stand and Madonna

programme.title: Roy Orbison at the BBC
programme.desc: A selection of archive performances for a celebration of the singer-songwriter, who Elvis Presley famously described as the best singer in the world. Featuring hits Only the Lonely, Crying and Oh, Pretty Woman, In Dreams and I Drove All Night

programme.title: Snooker: The World Championship
programme.desc: Seema Jaswal presents live coverage of the evening session on day eight at the Crucible Theatre in Sheffield, featuring the of conclusion of a second-round match and the opening session of another. Chinese stars Ding Junhui and Zhao Xintong could be involved in the match drawing to a close should they have won their respective first-round contests. After years of Ding being in contention to be the first player from his country to win this title, the honour fell to Zhao last year and he defeated another of his compatriots in Lei Peifan at this stage of the competition. With commentary and analysis from Steve Davis, Ken Doherty, Steven Hallworth, Stephen Hendry, John Parrott, Joe Perry, Dennis Taylor and Lee Walker

programme.title: Alex Higgins: The Peoples Champion
programme.desc: James Nesbitt narrates a look back at the often controversial life of the two-time world snooker champion, who was widely regarded as one of the most naturally gifted players in the history of his sport, but whose brilliance was flawed by his demons. With contributions by members of his family, as well as fellow snooker greats Jimmy White, Ronnie OSullivan, Steve Davis and Dennis Taylor

programme.title: Gold Run (2022)
programme.desc: Wartime drama telling the true story of how 55 tons of gold was transported through rough winter landscapes on trucks, trains and boats? by a group of unlikely resistance fighters ahead of the invading Nazi forces.? Starring Jon Oigarden, Thorbjorn Harr and Anatole Taubman. In Norwegian

programme.title: Gershwins Summertime: The Song That Conquered the World
programme.desc: How George Gershwin and DuBose Heywards song Summertime became a standard around the world. The film tells the story of its first appearance in the 1935 opera Porgy and Bess, charts how it has been reinterpreted by artists including Billie Holiday, Janis Joplin, Mahalia Jackson, Miles Davis and Ella Fitzgerald, and explores the many meanings that have been derived from it through the decades. Narrated by Pauline Black

programme.title: Why the Industrial Revolution Happened Here
programme.desc: Jeremy Black examines one of the most extraordinary periods in English history, explaining the unique economic, social and political conditions that led to the nation becoming the richest and most powerful on Earth by the 19th century. He traces the unprecedented explosion of new ideas and technological inventions that transformed an agrarian society into an increasingly industrial and urbanised one

programme.title: Alex Higgins: The Peoples Champion
programme.desc: James Nesbitt narrates a look back at the often controversial life of the two-time world snooker champion, who was widely regarded as one of the most naturally gifted players in the history of his sport, but whose brilliance was flawed by his demons. With contributions by members of his family, as well as fellow snooker greats Jimmy White, Ronnie OSullivan, Steve Davis and Dennis Taylor

programme.title: Snooker: The World Championship
programme.desc: Seema Jaswal presents live coverage of the evening session on day nine at the Crucible Theatre in Sheffield, featuring the second and penultimate sessions of two second-round matches. Should they have both won their first-round encounters, Ronnie OSullivan and John Higgins will continue their long-standing rivalry this evening. The two players have won this competition 11 times between them, with seven of those titles going to OSullivan. With commentary and analysis from Steve Davis, Ken Doherty, Steven Hallworth, Stephen Hendry, John Parrott, Joe Perry, Dennis Taylor and Lee Walker

programme.title: Arena
programme.desc: Orchestral concert film, taking a journey along rivers around the world accompanied by music composed by Richard Tognetti and William Barton. Footage from different landscapes reveal how rivers have shaped the world, creating caverns, valleys and fertile plains, and revealing how they have shaped the history of human civilisation, and been affected by it in turn. Narrated by Willem Dafoe

programme.title: The Riviera: A History in Pictures
programme.desc: Richard E Grant concludes his exploration of the French Rivieras artistic legacy by focusing on how art and celebrity became intertwined in the early part of the 20th century. Resident artists such as Picasso and Matisse found the sun-drenched landscape ideal for experimenting with their work, and as the region became a creative hub, the likes of novelist Jean Cocteau and ballet impresario Sergei Diaghilev were drawn to the area. This artistic community also liked to party on the Cote dAzurs beaches - a pastime that proved equally irresistible to the American multimillionaires arriving in the area on board transatlantic liners

programme.title: Art That Made Us
programme.desc: The splintering that occurred under the Stuart kings, as political, religious and cultural divisions fuelled war across three kingdoms. Architect Amanda Levete climbs the Tulip Stairs in the Queens House, Greenwich, and reassesses Inigo Jones elegant and innovative design, while portrait artist Tai Shan Schierenberg encounters Van Dycks monumental portrait of the Earl of Pembrokes family, and finds signs of the dysfunction and tensions which point to the civil war to come

programme.title: Genius of the Ancient World
programme.desc: Bettany Hughes turns her attention to Socrates, detailing how the Athenian philosopher secured a reputation as an influential maverick. She also highlights how his contributions as one of the founders of Western philosophy did not please his detractors, as his outspoken defence of his beliefs ultimately led to his execution

programme.title: The Riviera: A History in Pictures
programme.desc: Richard E Grant concludes his exploration of the French Rivieras artistic legacy by focusing on how art and celebrity became intertwined in the early part of the 20th century. Resident artists such as Picasso and Matisse found the sun-drenched landscape ideal for experimenting with their work, and as the region became a creative hub, the likes of novelist Jean Cocteau and ballet impresario Sergei Diaghilev were drawn to the area. This artistic community also liked to party on the Cote dAzurs beaches - a pastime that proved equally irresistible to the American multimillionaires arriving in the area on board transatlantic liners

programme.title: Candyman (2021)
programme.desc: Artist Anthony McCoy and his art gallery director girlfriend Brianna move into the heavily gentrified Cabrini Green neighbourhood of Chicago, where the urban legend of the Candyman once struck fear into residents hearts. For his provocative new exhibition, Anthony plunders the old legend. Sequel to the 1992 horror movie, starring Yahya Abdul-Mateen II and Vanessa Williams

programme.title: Weather for the Week Ahead
programme.desc: A look ahead to the latest forecast

programme.desc: A round-up of the latest headlines

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Live
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt is joined by chefs Georgina Hayden and Richard Bainbridge, with guest Nish Kumar and the winner of the Great British Menu. Helen McGinn picks the wines

programme.title: Flaming Feasts
programme.desc: Wrexham has burst onto the global stage in recent years thanks to a Hollywood football story that has captured imaginations far beyond Wales. Chris Roberts looks past the headlines to sample the city through the people who grow, brew and cook its food. His tour includes visits to Wrexham Lager Brewery, which was close to disappearing but has been revived by a group of locals, and Bellis Brothers Farm, a fifth-generation family business that began with a single strawberry crop in 1860. He also explores the citys evolving food scene at Harvest Vegetarian Kitchen and takes in the vibrant Portuguese community

programme.title: Football Focus
programme.desc: Alex Scott and guests look ahead to the weekends matches, including Manchester City v Southampton and Chelsea v Leeds United in the FA Cup semi-finals. Plus, news from the lunchtime Premier League game between Fulham and Aston Villa

programme.desc: Natasha Raskin Sharp presents the show from Shepton Mallet, Somerset, with the reds and blues led by John Cameron and Stephanie Connell

programme.title: London Marathon
programme.desc: Gabby Logan is joined by former world marathon record-holder Paula Radcliffe to look ahead to Sundays event, profiling contenders for the mens, womens and wheelchair races, and telling the stories of inspirational amateur runners

programme.desc: Hazel Irvine presents coverage of the afternoon session on day eight at the Crucible Theatre in Sheffield, featuring the opening session of one second-round match and the second session of another. programme.title: Match of the Day Live: The FA Cup
programme.desc: Manchester City v Southampton (kick-off 5.15pm). All the action from the opening semi-final at Wembley Stadium. City claimed an impressive 4-0 win over Liverpool in the quarter-finals, with Erling Haaland scoring a hat-trick, and are the favourites to go one better than last season and lift the cup. However, Championship outfit Southampton have already caused a major upset in the last eight, overcoming Arsenal 2-1 and are now looking to reach the final for the first time since 2003

programme.title: Blankety Blank
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh hosts the revival of the comedy quiz show, in which Fred Sirieix, Laura Whitmore, Eddie Kadi, Josh Widdicombe, Vanessa Feltz and Scarlett Moffatt fill in the blanks to help contestants win some brilliant prizes

programme.title: The Weakest Link
programme.desc: Romesh Ranganathan is joined by contestants Aisling Bea, La Voix, Ben Bailey Smith, Anna Richardson, Samantha Kinghorn, Will Best, Jason Byrne and Jacqui Joseph. They must initially work together to answer general knowledge questions and bank as much money as possible for their chosen charities, before voting off round by round

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: A mystery virus sweeps across Holby. Flynn is confronted with his old CO from the army, and works with Byron to restore order when an accident causes a blackout. Sarah questions Dylans commitment to fatherhood in light of the secrets he is keeping, Stevie refuses counselling in the aftermath of her recent loss, and Cam struggles to cope on his return to work

programme.title: Not Going Out
programme.desc: Lee fails in his bid to buy tickets to see his favourite band Oasis in concert, after briefly leaving his tablet in Lucys possession and somehow losing his place in the queue. A year on, he still cant come to terms with the disappointment. Lucy suggests a night in watching Oasis on the television instead, alongside an Indian takeaway. However, Lee cannot get over a feeling hes had for a long time - that it was entirely Lucys fault that he missed out. Comedy, starring Lee Mack and Sally Bretton

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Action from Manchester City v Southampton at Wembley Stadium in the semi-finals of the FA Cup, and Premier League matches, including Arsenal v Newcastle United at Emirates Stadium. The Saints were looking for a second successive major scalp in the competition after overcoming Arsenal in the previous round. Meanwhile, the Gunners continued their title challenge against an inconsistent Newcastle side. Plus, Fulham v Aston Villa, Liverpool v Crystal Palace, West Ham United v Everton and Wolverhampton Wanderers v Tottenham Hotspur

programme.title: London Marathon
programme.desc: Gabby Logan presents live coverage of the 46th staging of the 26.2-mile race, as approximately 59,000 competitors of all abilities take to the streets of the capital. Ethiopias Tigst Assefa and Sabastian Sawe of Kenya won the respective womens and mens elite races last year and the wheelchair races were won by Swiss athletes Marcel Hug and Catherine Debrunner. With commentary by Andrew Cotter, Steve Cram, Paula Radcliffe and Tanni Grey-Thompson, and reports from JJ Chalmers, Abby Cook, Tim Warwood and Harry Aikines-Aryeetey

programme.title: Sunday Politics Northern Ireland
programme.desc: Mark Carruthers talks to Sinn Fein president Mary Lou McDonald after her partys Ard Fheis in Belfast. Professor Jon Tonge from the University of Liverpool assesses Sinn Feins electoral chances

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: Music and worship from around the country

programme.title: Lifeline
programme.desc: Appeal on behalf of Action for ME, a charity that provides support and advocacy to people affected by ME, while looking to create positive change through medical research

programme.title: Despicable Me 2 (2013)
programme.desc: A reformed criminal mastermind who has abandoned his wicked ways for a life of fatherhood and jam-making helps a secret agent to bring one of his fellow evil geniuses to justice. The villain in question has stolen a dangerous experimental serum with the power to turn harmless animals into ravening monsters. Animated comedy sequel, with the voices of Steve Carell and Kristen Wiig

programme.title: Trace, Track, Get My Car Back
programme.desc: Tracker Shaquille is desperate to hunt down a car before its smuggled out of the country by an eastern European gang of car thieves and shipped to Romania. Meanwhile, in Guildford, tracker Richard is searching for a digger thats been hidden on the edge of a golf course

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: A round-up of the latest headlines

programme.title: Weather
programme.desc: The latest forecast

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.sub-title: Vale of Evesham: Bridging the Hungry Gap
programme.desc: Adam Henson and Sammi Kinghorn head to the Vale of Evesham, where fertile soils and a mild micro-climate have long made it one of Britains most productive growing regions, with a long history of producing food even in leaner months. Meanwhile, Charlotte Smith investigates how falling milk prices are leading some dairy farmers to call it a day, despite record exports and booming milk production

programme.title: Secret Garden
programme.desc: David Attenborough reveals the animals that have made a home in a woodland garden hidden at the bottom of a steep-sided Welsh valley. A tawny owl hunts for rodents drawn to the garden bird feeders, while a dormouse hibernates beneath the fallen leaves. In spring, a queen buff-tailed bumblebee emerges from a winter underground and prepares to build a new colony and a badger mother brings her young above ground for the first time

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the show from Beaumaris Castle on the island of Anglesey, where two antiques reveal how Welsh culture and language have been both suppressed and celebrated over the years. Other finds include a collection of carved wooden dogs and two pieces of Faberge jewellery which would have graced the highest levels of the pre-revolutionary Russian aristocracy

programme.title: The Cage
programme.desc: Crime drama, starring Sheridan Smith and Michael Socha as two casino employees who catch each other trying to steal from their employer. They agree to both abandon their plans, but as their lives spiral out of control, the pact theyve made frays, setting them on a high-stakes crash course with gangsters, police, and each other

programme.title: Match of the Day: The FA Cup
programme.desc: Chelsea v Leeds United. Action from the second semi-final at Wembley Stadium . The Blues thrashed Port Vale 7-0 in the quarter-finals and are looking to lift the trophy for the first time since 2018. Leeds only triumph in this competition was back in 1972 and they defeated West Ham United on penalties in the previous round

programme.title: The Womens Football Show
programme.desc: Highlights of the weekends games in the Super League, including Everton v Chelsea, Brighton and Hove Albion v Manchester City and Tottenham Hotspur v Manchester United

programme.title: The Claudia Winkleman Show
programme.desc: Claudia presents highlights from her first series, featuring appearances from Lisa Kudrow, Jennifer Saunders, Niall Horan, Guz Khan, Jeff Goldblum, James McAvoy, Rita Wilson, Jamie Dornan, Anna Faris, Jimmy Carr, Ralph Fiennes, Rachel Zegler and Jamie Dornan

programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical discussion and expert advice for viewers

programme.title: Rip Off Britain
programme.sub-title: Home Improvement Company Lies Cost Me Thousands
programme.desc: Gloria Hunniford, Julia Somerville and Louise Minchin investigate the case of the homeowner who paid a company nearly £2,000 up-front for garden maintenance, but has nothing to show for it. In the first of a new series of How-To Guides, consumer expert Harry Kind explains how to find a tradesperson you can trust

programme.title: Only Child
programme.desc: Richard embarks on a journey to gather together some of Kens nearest and dearest for his surprise 75th birthday party. However, tracking down the elusive Brian - Kens oldest friend whom he lost touch with years ago - proves quite the challenge. Elsewhere, Richards self-taped audition for a major role is troublesome and hes lost for words when Emily admits shes going on a date with a guy called Dave. Comedy, starring Gregor Fisher and Greg McHugh

programme.title: Only Child
programme.desc: While Richard goes to great lengths to hide the fact hes Norway-bound for his big TV break, Ken busies himself planning the special spot to scatter his beloved Jeans ashes - even if that means a less-than-sporting response from Rod at the football ground. As well as being a decoy for Richards departure, Emily is on hand to offer advice when things go awry for the Pritchards. Comedy, starring Greg McHugh, Gregor Fisher and Amy Lennnox

programme.title: Two Pints of Lager and a Packet of Crisps
programme.desc: After being chatted up by Louises father, Jonny resolves to become more macho but things dont go as planned. Donna refuses to have a baby with Gaz, leaving him with only one option for getting the child he so desperately wants

programme.title: Stacey Dooley
programme.desc: Part two of two. As Stacey continues to follow the emotional journey of the young women who have reported rape and now trying to navigate the justice system, she learns to appreciate that the statistics are stark. Fewer than two per cent of reported sexual assaults result in conviction, though this increases to over 50 per cent for cases that proceed to trial. As their trials near, the women learn that credibility also plays a pivotal role

programme.title: Stacey Dooley
programme.desc: Invited into the traditionally private Gypsy community, Stacey discovers the complex balancing act young women face growing up in one of Britains most maligned ethnic minorities. In Bedfordshire she meets 23-year-old Chantelle, who prides herself in keeping with the old Gypsy values her grandmother taught her and shares them on social media, where she has nearly 400,000 TikTok followers. In Lincolnshire, the reporter meets 18-year-old Serena, who is planning her wedding to her fiance Marco. He is also from a Gypsy and Traveller community and Serena wouldnt have it any other way

programme.title: Stacey Dooley
programme.desc: In Britain shoplifting is at an all-time high, incidents of violence and abuse against shop staff have skyrocketed, and a third of Brits have admitted to stealing through the self-checkouts. To understand this apparent high-street epidemic, Stacey heads to Manchester to meet the people behind the crime and spends time on the shop floor with the staff trying to prevent further thefts

programme.title: Doctor Who
programme.desc: Countless alien forces are drawn to a quiet backwater planet by a mysterious signal beaming out through time and space. The Doctor rescues Clara from a family Christmas dinner to help him discover what this message means, only to find that this insignificant planet has a terrible future, and that it falls to him to defend its peaceful inhabitants from the universes deadliest creatures. Matt Smith makes his final appearance as the Doctor in the 2013 Christmas special

programme.title: Ghosts
programme.desc: As Mike and Alison head off to celebrate Christmas with Mikes parents, the ghosts are touched to discover that Alison has left a gift for each of them in the library, with strict instructions not to open them until the couple return. Touched by her generosity, the spectres are inspired to give her a special Christmas present in return - but with Alison due back in a matter of hours, can they pull off their ambitious plan in time?

programme.title: Ghosts
programme.desc: Christmas is just around the corner and Mikes mum Betty is staying - and testing Mike and Alisons patience with her interfering ways. Theres no easy way to ask her to go home so the couple - and the ghosts - struggle on, with Button House feeling fuller than ever. Now a convert, Robin is missing that all-important Christmassy feeling, so the gang members share their secrets to getting into the festive spirit - but when even more decorations and merry music dont do the trick, the ghosts wonder if Robin will be able to capture that Christmas magic again

programme.title: Miranda
programme.desc: Socially awkward joke-shop owner Miranda is asked out to dinner by an old friend, prompting a visit to a clothing store to buy an outfit suitable for the date - but the expedition ends in disaster. First episode of the comedy, starring Miranda Hart, Sarah Hadland, Patricia Hodge and Sally Phillips. First aired in 2009

programme.title: Miranda
programme.desc: In a bid to encourage Gary to see her in a different light, Miranda joins an evening class to learn French, but is horrified to discover it is run by her former school teacher. The hapless singleton then decides a tango class is a more romantic option, only for her plans to take another turn for the worse. Comedy, guest starring Peter Davison, with Miranda Hart and Sarah Hadland

programme.title: Only Child
programme.desc: Bit-part actor and only child Richard travels back from London to his family home in the Highlands to visit his elderly father Ken a year on from the passing of his mum. However, he soon realises his dad might be in need of a bit more full-time help, not that eccentric, strong-willed technophobe Ken would agree. Comedy, starring Gregor Fisher and Greg McHugh

programme.title: Only Child
programme.desc: Richard decides the time has come to declutter his fathers house of all his junk, but Ken has suspicions over his sons true motives. The arrival of local handyman Mr Oddjob, Richards former school adversary Craig, complicates matters further as chaos commences. Comedy, starring Gregor Fisher and Greg McHugh

programme.title: Only Child
programme.desc: Concerned that Ken is spending too much time on his own, Richard offers to take him to the local golf club, but his attempts to give his father a chance at being sociable are thwarted by officious clubhouse chairman Crawford. Before he knows it, Richard finds himself playing a much bigger part in integrating his dad back into the community than was originally intended

programme.title: Only Child
programme.desc: Worried about his dads health, Richard battles officious receptionists - and a stubborn Ken - to get him a same-day appointment with Dr Roach. Later, Richard gets all-star struck when he learns his acting idol Lydia Bartlett is in Forres filming, while Emily suggests he imparts his wisdom to the local am-dram group. Guest starring Samantha Spiro

programme.title: Stacey Dooley

programme.title: Two Pints of Lager and a Packet of Crisps
programme.desc: Donna regrets persuading Gaz to try tantric sex when he gets hooked on it, while Louise wants a new family and seems to know exactly where to find one. Jonny gets a job as a farmer and Janet increases her home security

programme.title: Doctor Who
programme.desc: Countless alien forces are drawn to a quiet backwater planet by a mysterious signal beaming out through time and space. The Doctor rescues Clara from a family Christmas dinner to help him discover what this message means, only to find that this insignificant planet has a terrible future, and that it falls to him to defend its peaceful inhabitants from the universes deadliest creatures. Matt Smith makes his final appearance as the Doctor in the 2013 Christmas special

programme.desc: In a bid to encourage Gary to see her in a different light, Miranda joins an evening class to learn French, but is horrified to discover it is run by her former school teacher. The hapless singleton then decides a tango class is a more romantic option, only for her plans to take another turn for the worse. programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Nugget stands up for himself. Priya tries to help Ravi. Lauren explores a new business venture

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Ravi is determined to keep his family together. Mark tries to help Lauren. Elaine and Ian attend a gala

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Suki starts to worry about the family. Max and Cindy grow closer. The Mitchells rally together

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Max and Cindy share a moment. Ravi finally gets the help he needs. Lauren and Mark team up. Sam gives Mark an idea

programme.title: Four Weddings and a Funeral (1994)
programme.desc: A London mans misfortunes in love look set to change when he meets an American beauty. However, the prospect of a transatlantic relationship succeeding seems slim, so the pair go their separate ways - only for him to realise he is unable to get her out of his mind. Richard Curtiss comedy, with Hugh Grant, Andie MacDowell, John Hannah, Charlotte Coleman, Kristin Scott Thomas, Simon Callow and James Fleet

programme.title: Matthew Perry and the Ketamine Queen
programme.desc: Amber Haque explores the relationship between the Friends actor and Los Angeles-based drug dealer and dual US-UK citizen Jasveen Sangha - dubbed the Ketamine Queen by US prosecutors - who was one of five people charged in connection with Perrys untimely death, including two doctors and Perrys personal assistant. She pleaded guilty to five federal counts, including one of distributing ketamine resulting in death

programme.title: Fake Friend: The Ticket Scammer
programme.desc: The case of a Somerset high-flyer who turned his charm and connections into tools of deception, conning his friends and hundreds of others, while living a life of glamour himself. Through the voices of his victims and event industry insiders, this documentary uncovers the high personal and financial cost of chasing exclusivity, and how easily even the most cautious can be deceived

programme.title: Louis Therouxs Altered States
programme.desc: The documentary-maker explores the changing face of US culture, beginning with a look at a movement in Portland, Oregon, that seeks to broaden how the world views marriage in a time of rising divorce rates, suggesting that monogamy is not essential and that polyamorous or open relationships can be equally fulfilling. However, while Louis meets many who have found happiness with multiple partners, he also discovers tales of heartbreak and jealousy

programme.title: My Mates a Bad Date
programme.desc: Oloni has her work cut out with Vicky as she helps her find her perfect match, hoping to change her behaviour and her unrealistic criteria for her suitors

programme.title: Ashley Cain: Into the Danger Zone
programme.desc: The former footballer heads deep into the Colombian jungle to find out why gold is now twice as valuable than cocaine to the countrys organised crime groups, meeting the young men drawn into this illegal venture, discovering the shocking health impact mining is having on them, as well as meeting the cartel gangsters who control almost every element of life in these remote regions

programme.title: Ashley Cain: Into the Danger Zone
programme.desc: Ashley heads to Sweden where he joins police at the front line of the ongoing gang war and meets with gang bosses in underground car parks. As trouble expands throughout the country, children as young as 13 are taking weapons and justice into their own hands, a photojournalist shows him their documentation of the murder scenes before he meets with a top gang leader for a training session involving pistol target practice in the dead of night

programme.title: Panorama
programme.sub-title: Antisemitism: Why British Jews Are Afraid - Panorama
programme.desc: In-depth current affairs report covering a story behind the headlines

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The finalists chosen by judges Tom Kerridge, Lorna McNee and Phil Wang cook a banquet for the big names of the British film industry in Liverpool to decide which of them will be voted 2026s champion of champions. The chefs need to work together to wow the guests with their depth of flavour and flawless presentation

programme.title: Deadly 60
programme.desc: Steve Backshall goes in search of bat-hunting boas, meets a team working to save the deadly but cute slow loris, and comes face to face with the king cobra

programme.title: Gardeners World
programme.desc: Monty Don adds a dash of Mediterranean flair and flavour by planting lemons in pots. Hes also planting in the woodland garden and potting up summer bulbs. Sue Kent sows tender crops, Jamie Butterworth pays tribute to the humble euphorbia and garden designer Rosemary Alexander showcases her private garden

programme.title: Snooker: The World Championship
programme.desc: Rishi Persad presents live coverage of the morning session on day eight at the Crucible Theatre in Sheffield, featuring the start of one second-round match and the concluding session of another. With commentary and analysis from Steve Davis, Ken Doherty, Steven Hallworth, Stephen Hendry, John Parrott, Joe Perry, Dennis Taylor and Lee Walker

programme.title: Natural World
programme.desc: Documentary following a research project at Edinburgh Zoo aimed at providing chimpanzees with the training necessary to shoot a film. Chimp specialist Betsy Herrelko tries to communicate with the primates using video technology, as they are given cameras and taught how to use them. The filmed results can be seen at the end of the programme. Stuart McQuarrie narrates

programme.title: Live Womens Six Nations
programme.desc: England v Wales (Kick-off 2.15pm). Coverage of the opening match in the third round of fixtures, held at Ashton Gate in Bristol. England claimed an emphatic 67-12 victory in Wales last year and went on to win another Grand Slam, while the Welsh finished at the bottom of the table, and the hosts also won here 46-10 when the sides met in the 2024 competition

programme.title: Final Score
programme.desc: Jason Mohammad presents a round-up of the results and this afternoons news from the Premier League, EFL and Scotland

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Paul Martin presents from Longleat in Wiltshire, where expert Claire Rawle spots an unusual Tibetan teapot among the offerings brought in for valuation and it leads to the discovery of similar artefacts. The goods all go under the hammer and with worldwide interest on the objects, there is the potential for a record-breaking sale. Meanwhile, Paul explores Longleat House to find out more about its Elizabethan architecture and controversial creator

programme.title: Murder on the Orient Express (1974)
programme.desc: An American businessman travelling on the Orient Express from Istanbul to Paris tries to hire Hercule Poirot to discover the source of the death threats that have been made against him. The sleuth refuses, but wakes up the following morning to learn the man has been stabbed to death while the train was held up by heavy snow - and there is no shortage of suspects among the passengers. Agatha Christie mystery, starring Albert Finney, Lauren Bacall, Ingrid Bergman, Sean Connery and Vanessa Redgrave

programme.title: Live Womens Six Nations
programme.desc: France v Ireland (kick-off 8.10pm). All the action from the concluding match in the third round of fixtures, held at Stade Marcel-Michelin. France have won the last eight meetings between the sides, including a 27-15 victory at Affidea Stadium last year and a 38-17 triumph in the last contest on French soil in 2024. Ireland also lost their first match on their travels in this seasons competition, losing out 33-12 to holders England, while their opponents this evening got off to a winning start with a 40-7 home win at Stade des Alpes

programme.title: Black British Music at the BBC: Volume 2
programme.desc: DJ Spoony presents a personal selection of archive performances in a celebration of some of the very best in black British music from over the decades. The playlist features 40 tracks from the worlds of pop, soul, ska, reggae, rap and garage, with artists including the likes of Seal, the Selecter, Soul II Soul, FKA Twigs, Miss Dynamite and Shirley Bassey.

programme.title: Snooker: World Championship Extra
programme.desc: Extended highlights of a second-round match from day eight at the Crucible Theatre in Sheffield

programme.title: Mint
programme.desc: The only daughter of a notorious criminal family is obsessed with the story of how her parents fell in love and hopes to find a great romance of her own. She finally thinks her luck has changed, but when she discovers who the man of her dreams is, she cannot be sure how her parents will react. Romantic crime drama, starring Emma Laird and Loyle Carner

programme.title: McCartney: The Hunt for the Lost Bass
programme.desc: The disappearance of Paul McCartneys original Hofner bass more than 50 years ago is one of rock n rolls great mysteries and this documentary tells the story of the instrument and the quest to find it. Featuring interviews with the former Beatle, his brother Mike, musicians Klaus Voormann and Elvis Costello, and roadies, journalists and fans who embarked on a mission to return the bass to its place in music history

Plus, Adam Henson reports on farmers who are pooling their resources

programme.title: Andi Olivers Fabulous Feasts
programme.desc: Andi helps Jasper Thompson throws a party with a Caribbean flavour, to launch a community cafe at a pioneering Bristol homeless project that turns shipping containers into temporary housing. The chef must put together a feast that draws on her and Jaspers own roots in Antigua and Jamaica, whilst paying tribute to Bristols wider Caribbean heritage, using local produce

programme.title: London Marathon
programme.desc: Gabby Logan presents further live coverage of the 46th staging of the 26.2-mile race, as many of the approximately 59,000 amateur runners cross the finish line. With commentary by Andrew Cotter, Steve Cram, Paula Radcliffe and Tanni Grey-Thompson, and reports from JJ Chalmers, Abby Cook, Tim Warwood and Harry Aikines-Aryeetey

programme.title: Live UEFA Womens Champions League
programme.desc: Arsenal v Lyon (Kick-off 3.30pm). All the action from the first leg of the semi-final, held at Emirates Stadium. Arsenal edged past London rivals Chelsea 3-2 on aggregate in the quarter-finals to maintain their chances of retaining the trophy. Lyon are record eight-time champions of Europe and scored three goals in extra time in the last eight to overcome VfL Wolfsburg 4-0 on the night and 4-1 over the two legs

programme.title: London Marathon
programme.desc: Gabby Logan presents action from the 46th staging of the 26.2-mile race, featuring the key moments from the elite races and a look at the stories of some of the record number of amateur runners. With commentary by Andrew Cotter, Steve Cram, Paula Radcliffe and Tanni Grey-Thompson, and reports from JJ Chalmers, Abby Cook, Tim Warwood and Harry Aikines-Aryeetey

programme.title: This Farming Life
programme.desc: The Williamsons go for glory at their local show on Lewis, where their best sheep compete with the cream of the islands crop. Lucy and Adams baby arrives just as the work load ramps up, while Callum and Zara head south for a farm safety awareness event to commemorate the stories of farmers and their families affected by accidents at work

programme.title: Colombia with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: The adventurer explores the country as 50 years of civil war draws to a close in the wake of a recently signed peace deal. He meets guerrilla fighters who have pledged to lay down arms, but also discovers a negative consequence of their disarming, as the paramilitary gangs that control the drugs trade grow in power. Simon travels through the country with coca farmers, who discuss the government support they will need in the turbulent times ahead, as poverty and land ownership becoming pressing issues

programme.title: Hell for Leather: The Story of Gaelic Football
programme.desc: The games profound connection to people and place, focusing on the fiercely contested Ulster final, which both divided and unified, and how Meath drew inspiration from its mythic past to finally overcome Dublin, aided by Seán Boylans legendary potions and lotions. The programme also recalls the time Mick ODwyer committed near heresy by coaching Kildare to beat his home country of Kerry and reach the 1988 All-Ireland final

programme.title: Inside Britains National Parks
programme.desc: The Northumberland National Park is characterised by a unique landscape of wild moors, gentle hills and deep valleys criss-crossed by clear rivers, with its western border adjoining Kielder, one of the largest man-made forests in Europe. This vast natural expanse is a haven for numerous bird species, including the osprey, once extinct in the UK, but now returning annually from Africa to breed, while the park is also rich in history, including extensive stretches of Hadrians Wall

programme.title: Sunday Politics Northern Ireland
programme.desc: Mark Carruthers talks to Sinn Fein president Mary Lou McDonald after her partys Ard Fheis in Belfast. Professor Jon Tonge from the University of Liverpool assesses Sinn Feins electoral chances

programme.title: The Chronicles of Armagh

programme.desc: Documentary exploring a century of change in Britain through the prism of Queen Elizabeth IIs life. Through archive and eyewitness reflections, the film explores how she led the monarchy through profound social, political and cultural change. With contributions from Helen Mirren, David Attenborough, Barack Obama, Tony Blair, and HM Queen Camilla

programme.title: Shift the Thrift
programme.desc: Joanna Page is in the West Yorkshire town of Hebden Bridge, where friends Emma and Sophie compete to raise money for Dove House Hospice, picking up items including a vintage St Michael jacket, a rare red rotary dial telephone, a 1970s Holly Hobbie doll and a colourful 1960s picnic set

programme.title: Secret Garden
programme.desc: David Attenborough reveals the hidden wild world of a country garden in Cumbria. A barn swallow returns to its birthplace to prepare a new nest, field mice sneak under the noses of predators, a newt lays eggs in the pond, and a mole barrels through his tunnel network in search of a female. But Cumbria is the wettest county in England, and when the weather breaks, things take a turn for the worse for the animals

programme.title: Match of the Day: The FA Cup
programme.desc: Chelsea v Leeds United. Action from the second semi-final at Wembley Stadium . The Blues thrashed Port Vale 7-0 in the quarter-finals and are looking to lift the trophy for the first time since 2018. Leeds only triumph in this competition was back in 1972 and they defeated West Ham United on penalties in the previous round

programme.title: Strange Evidence
programme.desc: Experts investigate footage of a van hurtling out of control across a roundabout, narrowly avoiding pedestrians, before smashing into a car. A concentrated jet of fire shot out its side, sending a ribbon of flame dozens of feet across the street

programme.title: UFO POV

programme.title: Ancient Aliens

programme.desc: Cameras in Texas capture a 42-foot spiders web capable of capturing a human. A Baltimore street collapses into a mysterious void which some experts believe are the remnants of legendary tunnels built during the War of 1812

programme.desc: The captains face major storm fronts, and are forced to choose between braving the elements or remaining in port until the conditions have cleared up

There are nine weeks left of the gold season to go and the pressure is on. At Swiss Diggings, an unemployed Donnie begs owner Dennis for a new job

programme.title: Ancient Aliens
programme.desc: The mysteries of the Aztecs, who built one of the worlds most powerful and feared empires. They claimed that they were guided by a pantheon of more than a thousand gods who came to Earth from different parts of the Universe

programme.title: The Proof Is Out There
programme.sub-title: UFO Over the Andes, the Ohio Grassman and Ancient Horn Mystery
programme.desc: The legendary Ohio Grassman is apparently captured in footage of a tall, dark-coloured, hairy creature walking through woods. Plus, a look at the Chilean family photograph of a real UFO flying above the Andes Mountains and the notion that a 5,000-year-old horn discovered on a beach in England belongs to a prehistoric creature that still lives

programme.title: The Proof Is Out There
programme.sub-title: Men in Black Calls, Old Kentucky Aliens and Mysterious Eclipse
programme.desc: Tony Harris and his team of experts examine several seemingly impossible events, such as a family managing to survive a bolt of lightning ripping through their car during a Kansas storm, a bizarre creature spotted on a driveway camera and an image captured of an alien probe

programme.title: What on Earth?
programme.desc: Documentary series focusing on six unfathomable mysteries - from a gigantic tsunami that never hits land, to a sinister blood-red lake in war-torn Baghdad

programme.title: What on Earth?
programme.desc: The series telling the stories behind remarkable satellite images continues, focusing on strange barren rings in the Namib Desert and a lost city in the heart of the Amazon

programme.title: What on Earth?
programme.desc: Photographs of a 25-mile structure known as the eye of the Sahara, and an extraordinary cloud emanating from an island in the Indian Ocean

programme.title: Cursed Histories
programme.desc: More instances of the granting of so-called curses. This episode highlights the Egyptian royals who promised turmoil for anyone who dares disturb their eternal rest. Plus, a devilish figure at the Mississippi crossroads who granted great musical talents in exchange for a bluesmans soul and the pantheon of gods with the apparent power to punish any accursed name carved into dark tablets of ancient lead

programme.title: Cursed Histories
programme.desc: Elements, places, icons or events that seem to be plagued by what can only be described as a curse. This episode focuses on a damned amethyst gem, a devilish beast and the downfall of a family for several generations.

programme.title: Secret Societies: In the Shadows
programme.desc: The murky history of secret societies, examining heir origins and their intent, assessing whether they intended to pull the levers of power or aim to destroy those in charge. The Knights Templar owned vast swathes of land, commanded fleets, and lent money to kings. This documentary seeks to separate the facts from the fiction

programme.title: Holy Marvels with Dennis Quaid
programme.desc: The Hollywood actor considers buildings of Biblical proportions. From a grand basilica in Vatican City and the towering monuments of Ancient Egypt to a Biblical tower and a lost city in the clouds of Peru, he uncovers their construction, purpose and the enduring mysteries they hold

programme.title: Field of Vampires
programme.desc: A plot in Poland containing 30 deviant burials. One woman was buried with a sickle across her neck and a padlock on her toe - this corpse was feared to be a real-life vampire. An international team of experts uses cutting-edge science and historical research to investigate who this woman was and why she was so feared

programme.title: Strange Evidence
programme.desc: How mourners in Central Asia were shocked to enter their village graveyard and find row upon row of holes. The graves were all empty - but where had the bodies gone? Plus, a car on a busy Moscow street that seemed to collide with an invisible object

programme.title: Paranormal: Caught on Camera
programme.desc: Documentary the most chilling and unexplainable paranormal activity footage captured by eyewitnesses

programme.title: National Park Mysteries
programme.desc: A look at Cleveland National Forest, where reports of gun-wielding ghosts, savage creatures and otherworldly portals are rife

programme.title: National Park Mysteries
programme.desc: A trip through the ginormous forest in Arizona. Strange encounters rumoured to have happened there include an alien abduction

programme.title: Alien Files: Unsealed
programme.desc: Alien encounters have offered a possible glimpse into the secrets of interstellar navigation. Giorgio Tsoukalos contemplates whether this might one day ensure mankinds survival

programme.title: Alien Files: Unsealed
programme.desc: Giorgio Tsoukalos reveals how advances in technology have given unprecedented access to distant galaxies that may harbour life. However, alien life may be found closer to home

programme.title: Alien Files: Unsealed
programme.desc: As the world moves into the digital age, more people are capturing images in the sky that they are unable to explain, leading to an influx of reported accounts of alien encounters

programme.title: Alien Files: Unsealed
programme.desc: For years many people have argued that UFOs are terrestrial in reality. Though they may be correct, it is unlikely to be in the way that they think they are

programme.title: Alien Files: Unsealed
programme.desc: The year 1947 saw an unprecedented wave of UFO sightings that propelled the phenomenon to the world stage. Giorgio Tsoukalos examines the US Air Forces findings

programme.title: Alien Files: Unsealed
programme.desc: For many people, the most credible UFO reports are those raised by police officers. This programme investigates the most significant reports from police records

programme.title: Alien Files: Unsealed
programme.desc: Between 1981 and 2011, cameras on Nasa space shuttles have picked up many images of UFOs that experts still struggle to explain

programme.title: Alien Files: Unsealed
programme.desc: While many UFO sightings occur in rural locations with only a single eyewitness, there have been sightings over cities witnessed by hundreds

programme.title: WWEs Greatest Moments
programme.desc: Unforgettable moments featuring WWE superstars, highlighting the tag teams of the 1990s

programme.title: WWEs Greatest Moments
programme.desc: Unforgettable moments featuring WWE superstars, with action covering the 1980s onwards

programme.title: Hardcore Pawn
programme.desc: Members of the Gold family open the doors to their huge Detroit pawn shop once again as they encounter intriguing finds and awkward customers. Here, the staff are pushed to their breaking point by a customers endless parade of worthless items. Later, Ashley gets the chance to assert herself

programme.title: Hardcore Pawn
programme.desc: The Gold family and its employees are ready to haggle as members of the public continue to bring in their unique items in the hope of raising some cash

programme.title: Hardcore Pawn
programme.desc: A customer wants to sell a coat allegedly made of rare monkey fur. Seth is tempted to make a purchase that could prove beneficial to the finances of the business

programme.title: Hardcore Pawn
programme.desc: The toss of a coin determines the value of a gold chain Les believes is out of fashion. A customer is upset when Seth prevents her from using the staff toilets

programme.title: Hardcore Pawn
programme.desc: A woman rejects custom-made earrings, as she thought the term diamond cut meant they were diamonds. Seth puts his faith into younger sellers, believing they can outperform Les

programme.title: Hardcore Pawn
programme.desc: An employee accidentally cracks the screen of a plasma-screen television. A customer becomes irate when the pneumatic hammer he is trying to sell does not work

programme.title: Junk and Disorderly
programme.desc: Henry and Skid snap up another electric haul at the Tewkesbury auction before heading to the Tettenhall Transport Museum

programme.title: Junk and Disorderly
programme.desc: Henry and Skid hit the road in search and automotive junk to sell, starting at the HJ Pugh auction

programme.title: Pawn Stars
programme.desc: Rick sees red when a Rolex Submariner hits the counter, Corey gets slap-happy over a hockey stick signed by Paul Newman, while Chum and Santos are sweet on a set of Mr Softee trading cards. Later, Rick heads to the range to see a set of Bolivian thunder mugs

programme.title: Pawn Stars
programme.desc: A seller brings a Mr T wall clock to the shop, Rick checks out James Bonds credit card from the 2006 movie Casino Royale, while Corey plays with an Atomic Cannon toy. Chum and Rick get down and dirty in Texas looking for fossil

programme.title: Auction Hunters
programme.desc: Allen Haff and Ton Jones bid for a 1970s dune buggy and a vintage pool table at a desert auction in Palm Springs, California

programme.title: Auction Hunters
programme.desc: Allen Haff and Ton Jones bid against inexperienced competitors for a vintage fishing seat at a container auction in San Diego

programme.title: Auction Hunters
programme.desc: Allen Haff and Ton Jones bid against a wealthy opponent in San Diego, and succeed in securing a 1950s baseball machine and a 1980s hydraulic rescue tool

programme.title: Container Wars
programme.desc: Rival bidders attend a competitive auction for the contents of shipping containers. In this war of serious money, sky-high stakes and outsize egos, its either win big or go home empty-handed

programme.title: Container Wars
programme.desc: Matt attends an auction in Oakland where he sets out to prove a point

programme.title: Container Wars
programme.desc: New arrival Penny joins the auctioneer bidding circuit - and soon has Israeli young guns Uzi and Schlomi seeing red

programme.title: Storage Wars
programme.sub-title: Piles to Go Before I Keep
programme.desc: Laura takes the reigns alone in Fullerton and proceeds to outsell Dan. Brandi gets assaulted by her locker but ultimately drives home a winner. Rene postpones a day date in favour of a work date, while Darrell stumbles across and unexpected collection

programme.title: Storage Wars
programme.desc: In Victorville, a friendly duel runs Dusty up, though his mother lode of vinyl might just save him. Ivys locker shines some light on the business, while Lisa learns that she needs her locker about as much as she needs a hole in her head

programme.title: Storage Wars
programme.sub-title: How the West Covina Was Won
programme.desc: In West Covina, Lisa is so busy looking back that she makes the worst auction mistake. Rene finds a bargain and really makes the most of it. Ivy unfolds his way to a profit and unloads some stuff onto Lisa

programme.title: Storage Wars
programme.desc: Rene shows up in Orange, declaring himself the King, while Dusty isnt quite ready to concede the title. Kenny accidentally re-invents the wheel, while Lisa sees things from a slightly different perspective

programme.title: Storage Wars
programme.desc: The units are packed full in Stanton, as Darrell has to deal with Kimbers eyes on a competitor. Ivy brings a full truck, hoping for a second haul. Dusty unpacks what could be the ultimate assistant, while Renes hunch serves him well

programme.title: Storage Wars
programme.desc: On an uncharacteristically wet day in Montebello, King Darrell is on a mission to defend his throne. However, Rene and Casey signal their intentions to undermine his plans. Kenny gets bowled over by his locker, while Lisa manages to rope herself something quite impressive

programme.title: Beyond Skinwalker Ranch
programme.desc: Andy and Paul return to Mount Shasta, California, to investigate the cause of the strange and potentially deadly energies that seem to draw everything toward the mountain - from people to Unidentified Anomalous Phenomena

programme.title: Curse of Skinwalker Ranch
programme.desc: After the team unearths new and bizarre clues from the Mesa on Skinwalker Ranch, a complex laser experiment leads to a shocking encounter

programme.title: A Ship Through Time
programme.desc: Part two of two. Sean Fletcher investigates the secrets of a shipwrecked 15th-century trading galleon found by accident off Newport in 2002, her international significance and how two of Europes most turbulent and violent wars shaped her fate. He discovers what this trading galleon carried, and to where and a possible powerful owner. He also sheds light on a possible connection to the Wars of the Roses

programme.title: The Witches of Salem
programme.desc: The hysteria that unfolded during the Salem witch trials, leading to the execution of 20 women in an affluent New England community. Mark Strong narrates a real-life horror story, with young teenage girls tragically accused and convicted by the shadowy adults lurking in the background. The series draws on extensive historical documents and court records to bring this drama to life

programme.title: Historys Greatest Mysteries
programme.desc: An ancient mechanical device found on a 2,000-year-old shipwreck has become known as the worlds first computer. The machine is centuries more advanced than historians thought possible, but its origin and true purpose are shrouded in secrecy

programme.title: WWEs Greatest Moments
programme.desc: Unforgettable moments featuring WWE superstars, with action covering the 1980s onwards

programme.title: The Proof Is Out There
programme.sub-title: UFO Over the Andes, the Ohio Grassman and Ancient Horn Mystery
programme.desc: The legendary Ohio Grassman is apparently captured in footage of a tall, dark-coloured, hairy creature walking through woods. Plus, a look at the Chilean family photograph of a real UFO flying above the Andes Mountains and the notion that a 5,000-year-old horn discovered on a beach in England belongs to a prehistoric creature that still lives

programme.title: The Proof Is Out There
programme.sub-title: Men in Black Calls, Old Kentucky Aliens and Mysterious Eclipse
programme.desc: Tony Harris and his team of experts examine several seemingly impossible events, such as a family managing to survive a bolt of lightning ripping through their car during a Kansas storm, a bizarre creature spotted on a driveway camera and an image captured of an alien probe

programme.title: The Proof Is Out There
programme.sub-title: Teen UFO, Flying Dinosaur Over Idaho, and Headless Goats
programme.desc: A photograph taken by a Swedish teenager said to offer some of the most convincing UFO evidence ever. Plus, a video posted online showing a pterodactyl-like creature flying over Idaho and the reason headless goats are repeatedly being dumped in an Atlanta River

programme.title: Britains Most Historic Towns
programme.desc: Alice Roberts tells the story of Viking England by studying the history of York. She takes part in a Viking battle, meets the metal detectorists behind one of the UKs greatest archaeological finds and gets her hands on an almost 1,000-year-old poo. Aerial archaeologist Ben Robinson takes to the skies to see how the current layout of York reflects its history

programme.title: Britains Most Historic Towns
programme.desc: Alice Roberts explores the Norman period of British history by examining events that occurred in Winchester, a historic town that still retains many hallmarks of the 11th and 12th centuries. To get a taste of this era, she attempts to storm a castle wearing chain mail, investigates the origins of modern surgery, and tries some eel pie. At the conclusion of the episode, cutting edge CGI is used to take the town back in time, and recreate how it would have looked during this period of British history

programme.title: Dickinsons Real Deal
programme.desc: David Dickinson and the team of antiques dealers visit Barnsley in South Yorkshire, where Tim Hogarth assesses a collection of vinyl records and Helen Gardiner is pleased to appraise a Lorenzl figurine

programme.desc: Quiz show hosted by Bradley Walsh. Pete, Sheila, Maisie and Noel must pit their wits against the Chaser, a ruthless quiz genius determined to stop them winning the cash prize.

programme.title: Tenable
programme.desc: Quiz hosted by Warwick Davis in which a team from Cumbria answers questions about top 10 lists from the realms of pop culture and general knowledge, then tries to score a perfect 10 in the final round to take home the prize money

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: Five dealers bid against each other to buy four new items - a vintage bucket with a dark past, a beautiful jewellery box, two chairs that bamboozle them and an antique foot warmer

programme.title: Babylon (2022)
programme.desc: Film assistant Manuel Torres yearns to rise through the ranks in 1920s Hollywood, starting with a lowly position working for matinee idol Jack Conrad. Manny is smitten with ingenue Nellie LaRoy, who intends to achieve big screen fame. Their fates entwine with a motley crew of wannabes and hangers on. Writer/director Damien Chazelles comedy drama, starring Brad Pitt, Margot Robbie and Diego Calva

programme.title: Taskmaster
programme.desc: The contestants are treated to a banger of a song sung by Little Alex Horne, but some of them dont entirely appreciate it. Kumail Nanjiani holds Amy Gledhill and Joel Dommett while they ask him questions about shapes and colour, Joanna Page reveals her favourite childhood tipple and Armando Iannucci goes for an ice cream

programme.title: Pearl (2022)
programme.desc: In 1918 Texas, teenager Pearl dreams of becoming a dancer in Hollywood in direct opposition to her domineering German immigrant mother. The projectionist of the local cinema fans the flames of the teenagers rebellion and mother and daughter clash violently. Horror, starring Mia Goth and David Corenswet

programme.title: Travel Man: 48 Hours In
programme.desc: Comedian Mawaan Rizwan joins Joe Lycett to explore Dublin. Ten statues across the city have been given QR codes. When visitors scan the code, the statue phones them, so Joe and Mawaan get to hear from Oscar Wilde and Molly Malone. After exploring the citys smallest restaurant, they head to its largest tourist attraction, the Guinness Storehouse, where they learn how to pour the perfect pint and even get their faces printed on it. Day two begins with a swim at Forty Foot, then at Windmill Lane studios the duo learn the story of Irelands answer to Abbey Road and lay down a track of their own

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The first visit of the week is to Wisteria House, Hampshire, where owner Jane Faulkner invites guests to her establishment. Later, the group members try their hands at milking a cow, while 25-year-old Zak causes surprises when he reveals his years of hospitality experience

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The guests visit The Living Room BandB in Bournemouth on their second night, which is co-owned by 25-year-old Zak Busby-Pelekanou. While at the establishment, Mike and Charlie have a hairy issue, and Jane and Amy encounter a spider, before getting to grips with an ambitious breakfast menu

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The third visit of the week is to the Valley Hotel in Anglesey, where owner Mike Lewis hopes his five-star pedigree will help him reap dividends in the competition. After a visit to a local RNLI station, guests Jane and Amy are left feeling sluggish when they spot an unwanted visitor in their room, and their criticism leaves the hosts looking for answers

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: David and Elvira Barratt welcome the contestants to Barratts at Tyn Rhyl, Denbighshire, but the group has an unpleasant experience on arrival. The guests take part in an activity at a local cookery school where they are taught the art of preparing and cooking sheeps testicles. However, come feedback time, the hosts are left feeling distraught

programme.title: Four in a Bed
