programme.title: 24 Hours in AandE
programme.desc: Three-year-old Louie is airlifted to St Georges after being run over by a car while playing outside, with doctors expressing concern about his neck, which seems to be stuck in one position. Elsewhere, 14-year-old Josh is treated after injuring his ankle when riding a BMX with his father on the front. Meanwhile, 41-year-old Sam arrives in resus with a cut to her head and possible spinal and internal injuries after losing control of her car and crashing into a tree

programme.title: Food Unwrapped
programme.desc: Kate Quilton heads to Kenya to see how cold-brew coffee differs from iced coffee. Helen Lawal tries frozen pastries in Portugal to see how well they perform well against fresh, while Briony May Williams heads to Staffordshire to learn how parsnips are cultivated to taste so sweet

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The competition heads to Anglesey, where best pals Richard Tuke and Susan Hatch hope to show that Blackthorn Farm BandB, which they own, is better than any of their guests establishments. However, they find that their close friendship is scrutinised even more than their property

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The White Swan Hotel in Yeadon, West Yorkshire, is the second stop of the competition, and owners Graham and Barbara Yellow hope their basic facilities and affordable prices will please their guests. Spiritual couple Malcolm and Fiona Piers-Taylor get good vibes from their room, Rachel Lonergan views the hosts in a new light and breakfast is well received by the contestants, despite having to pay a supplement fee

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Vegetarians Malcolm and Fiona Piers-Taylor host at the Old Mill House BandB in Bridport, Dorset, and both come under attack at dinner when Malcolm voices his opinions on meat eating. In the morning, a meditation session gets a mixed response, and most of the guests are left cold by the vegetarian buffet breakfast

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Rachel and Dan Lonergan welcome their guests to the Brighton Inn, East Sussex, where they hope to offer a luxury, boutique experience. Malcolm and Fiona Piers-Taylor give their room a thorough inspection, before the groups learns the art of building a human pyramid in the afternoons activity. At breakfast, they discover a lack of vegetarian alternatives, and the hosts suspect game-playing when they are shocked by some negative feedback

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The BandB owners come together to confront their rivals and find out how much they have been paid. Richard and Susan are keen to tackle the bathroom issues of Barbara and Graham, who bask in the comments from Malcolm and Fiona. The Old Mill House proprietors defend their vegetarian breakfast routine, but serve up snobby feedback to Rachel and Dan from the Brighton Inn

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Leah Charles-King comes to the help of Kerry and Stewart from Newcastle who, with five children and eight grandchildren, are looking forward to finding a little oasis on the Canary island of Lanzarote that they can all escape to. Leah lines up five properties for thepair to consider within their budget of £120,000

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Danni Menzies comes to the aid of husband and wife Paul and Tracy, who are hunting around the villages of Lake Vinuela in southern Spain for their dream holiday home

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Kevin McCloud meets actor Sean Simons, who has bought the ruins of Cloontykilla Castle in Co Roscommon with a view to using them to create a spectacular mock 16th-century home. However, the outlandish plans - including Jacuzzis in the battlements and water-spouting gargoyles - have Kevin worried the project could become a garish nightmare, and his scepticism increases when he realises Sean has not consulted an architect. Disaster seems to be looming as the contractors grow frustrated and planning regulations are ignored, but somehow things slowly begin to take shape - until a national economic crisis plunges everything into chaos

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Kevin McCloud meets artists Indi Waterstone and her partner Rebecca, who are planning to construct a modern, larch-clad home on the Isle of Skye, with a second hand-crafted building alongside to use as their studio, all on a tight budget of £150,000. They have been saving for years, but making it happen will mean taking on the savage weather, not to mention the sceptical locals - and as if that werent enough, most of the work is being done by one man, local builder Donald

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Oil and gas co-ordinator Danielle Greenwood is keen to impress as she hosts the first dinner party in the Merseyside area, as she welcomes divorcee Jade Chantelle Short, Michael Buble impersonator Jamie Somers, pastoral care manager Steven Booth, and nursery school teacher Anna Senior into her home. Danielle takes no risks with a down-to-earth menu, which she hopes will help bag her the £1,000 prize at the end of the week

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Pastoral care manager Steven Booth hosts the second Merseyside dinner party, and names each course after a Tina Turner song, hoping to win the £1,000 prize. The food is well received, but tensions rise when one guest makes a tactless comment

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Glamorous PR representative Jade Chantelle hosts the third night in Merseyside, where she hopes a casino-themed evening with lashings of champagne and a high-class menu will help her to win the £1,000 prize

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Primary school teacher Anna Senior hosts the fourth night in Merseyside with a fairy tale-themed evening. Her menu consists of a Mad Hatters soup and sandwich, a special pork dish cooked three ways and baked cheesecake for dessert, but she must try to control the tension between Jamie and Jade to have a successful party

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Michael Buble impersonator Jamie Somers hosts the final Merseyside dinner party, but may have to put aside his differences with guest Jade if he is to be crowned the winner of the £1,000 cash prize

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Inexperienced father-and-daughter team Steve and Elise host the weeks first visit, at 35 Main Street in Pembrokeshire, offering guests a visit to a local brewery

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: For the weeks second visit, Anjana and David are aiming to get their guests to branch out at Chillderness, their unique off-grid site in Powys, complete with tree-top accommodation. Alas, the outdoor toilets mean some of the BandBers are far from keen to lay down roots. And although everyone has a hoot on a trip to meet some birds of prey, the self-catering breakfast leads to some unsavoury feedback

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: At Redberth Gardens in Tenby, hosts Carys and Rhys get off to a flyer thanks to the facilities at their glampsite, which include hot tubs, cinema screens and outdoor table football. But tensions soon emerge during a trip to the zoo, as two BandBers cant help but revisit past comments

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The final visit is to Tanya and Garys Bae Abermaw BandB, in Barmouth, Gwynedd. Theyre hoping their incredible views will put them in sight of the winners plaque - but its the vista looking into the room rather than out that doesnt delight one set of guests. Can an impressive display at breakfast turn things around?

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: After an eventful week in Wales, the BandBers meet up once more to debate their differences and learn who has won. Will some lingering vendettas spoil the party?

programme.title: 24 Hours in AandE
programme.desc: Fifty-two-year-old retired firefighter John is airlifted to AandE after being accidentally hit in the face with a golf club, which hit him so hard that it snapped. With a suspected critical head injury, he is sent for a CT scan, and doctors are concerned that his right eye is not reacting as it should and he is weak down the right-hand side of his body. Meanwhile, 77-year-old Joan arrives in St Georges after falling down the stairs at home and hurting her leg. As she waits to be treated, she talks about growing up in Camberwell

programme.title: 24 Hours in AandE
programme.desc: After falling over during the night at home and being rushed into AandE, 85-year-old Maura faces potentially life-changing injuries. CT scans reveal broken bones in her spine, and her son and daughter-in-law must decide when to share the gravity of the situation with Maura and her granddaughter Meg. Meanwhile, 37-year-old doctor Jim arrives at St Georges after a collision with a van while he was cycling to work. Despite putting on a brave face, scans reveal his injuries are more serious than first thought. Plus, a chef is treated after cutting her thumb while chopping peppers at home

programme.title: 24 Hours in AandE
programme.desc: Train driver Andy, 48, is rushed in after falling 12ft from a tree while cutting branches and landing on his feet, badly fracturing both ankles, and doctors are concerned the impact may have caused life-changing damage to critical nerves and blood vessels in his feet and legs. Angela, 59, has come to St Georges as her heart has been beating at over 150bpm for eight hours, risking stroke and other complications. Cardiologist Dr Aruns attempts to bring down her pulse using a powerful drug arent successful, so he decides to electrically shock her heart to reset it

programme.title: Emergency Helicopter Medics
programme.desc: In Oxfordshire, cyclist Matt suffers life-threatening injuries after a collision with a car. On the A1, 64-year-old John is trapped in his car after a head-on collision. Meanwhile, in Cumbria, heli-medics fly to one of the most remote roads in Britain to help Caroline who has come off a motorbike and is injured

programme.title: 24 Hours in AandE
programme.desc: Fifty-two-year-old retired firefighter John is airlifted to AandE after being accidentally hit in the face with a golf club, which hit him so hard that it snapped. With a suspected critical head injury, he is sent for a CT scan, and doctors are concerned that his right eye is not reacting as it should and he is weak down the right-hand side of his body. Meanwhile, 77-year-old Joan arrives in St Georges after falling down the stairs at home and hurting her leg. As she waits to be treated, she talks about growing up in Camberwell

programme.title: 24 Hours in AandE
programme.desc: After falling over during the night at home and being rushed into AandE, 85-year-old Maura faces potentially life-changing injuries. CT scans reveal broken bones in her spine, and her son and daughter-in-law must decide when to share the gravity of the situation with Maura and her granddaughter Meg. Meanwhile, 37-year-old doctor Jim arrives at St Georges after a collision with a van while he was cycling to work. Despite putting on a brave face, scans reveal his injuries are more serious than first thought. Plus, a chef is treated after cutting her thumb while chopping peppers at home

programme.title: Food Unwrapped
programme.desc: In Lincolnshire, Kate Quilton sees whether shallots are actually just small onions and visits a potato farm looking for the reason baking potatoes cost more. Matt Tebbutt heads to frozen food company McCain to see how potatoes are transformed into their various products, most notably chips. Helen Lawal is in Spain to find out why it is possible to get sheeps cheese in the supermarket, but never sheeps milk

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Shop manager Nicola Steele hosts the first dinner party in Cardiff, and hopes that her dog-themed surprises and unusual entertainment will impress her guests - army wife Rachael Smith, casting agency owner Jerry Lockett, childminder Andrea Grainger and chatterbox Sam Clark - and win her the £1,000 cash prize

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Jerry Lockett hosts the second dinner party in Cardiff, and following a flood at his home, hopes to impress his guests by serving the meal at the grand venue of his cousins mansion

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Army wife Rachael Smith hosts the third party in Cardiff, allowing her competitive edge and no-nonsense approach to come to the fore in her bid to pick up the £1,000 prize

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Costume designer Sam Clarke prepares a Mexican menu for the fourth dinner party in Cardiff. Everything does not go to plan, however, when two guests begin fighting at the table, and his fun game leaves everyone in tears

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Childminder Andrea Grainger hosts the last dinner party as the competition in Cardiff draws to a close. She hopes a menu of traditional Welsh dishes will help her secure the prize, and lines up a selection of games to improve her chances of success. As the evening comes to an end, the diners discover which of the meals made the best impression

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Married couple Mo and Anthony seek a holiday apartment with a sea view in Torremolinos on Spains Costa del Sol. Laura Hamilton is tasked with showing them the best of what their £130,000 budget can get them. However, whether any of the five properties the property expert has up her sleeve will result in an offer remains to be seen

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Natalie and Matt from north Wales enlist the help of Jean Johansson in their search for a holiday home in Caleta de Fuste, Fuerteventura with a budget of £75,000

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Retirees Kay and Barry have decided its time to fulfil their dream of buying a holiday home in the historic city of Jerez in southern Spain. With a budget of £105,000, they want an apartment close to all the action. Jasmine Harman lines up five excellent properties for the pair to choose between

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Danni Menzies is in the beautiful coastal region of Puglia in the south of Italy with house-hunter Marion, whos dreaming of buying a holiday home she can enjoy with her teenage daughter, friends and family. With her best friend Bronagh on hand to lend support, Marion would love to find a three-bedroom villa close to the beach - can Danni deliver her dream for Marions budget of £120,000?

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Shane and Sharon dream of owning a holiday home on the Costa del Sol that they can escape to during the winter to enjoy the areas all-year round outdoor lifestyle. With a budget of £135,000, they want an apartment or town house close to the beach around La Cala de Mijas, with two bedrooms so their family can come to visit. Danni Menzies rises to the challenge to find them the perfect property

programme.title: Love It or List It
programme.desc: A special episode in which two new property experts take the reins - siblings, business partners and experienced developers Scarlette and Stuart Douglas, who head to Ipswich to help a family living in a four-bed semi. City-loving Norbert loves the bedroom sizes, the neighbours, the location and the convenient commute to work, but Shammie hates the busy road, small kitchen and only having one bathroom. Armed with £30,000, Stuart comes up with a masterplan to create a more sociable family space downstairs, with real wow factor, and also give them a much-needed second bathroom upstairs

programme.title: George Clarkes Remarkable Renovations
programme.desc: In Wales, George meets Tim and Kirsti, who are embarking on a renovation to create a new home for their blended family. Tims two children recently moved over from South Africa, making their current rental tight on space, so they needed to upsize and quickly. The high-street-shop-turned-accountants-office offered the five bedrooms they needed for a price they could afford. However, it lacks a family kitchen as well as connection to the garden, so they need to build a modern extension. But the building was built in 1725, is listed and is in a conservation area, which doesnt make things simple

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The competition begins at Lodge Duxford in Cambridgeshire, where a trip to see some vintage RAF planes sends group relations soaring, but two guests give poor cleanliness scores

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Jo and Annabel welcome the BandBers to their modern aparthotel Emerald Stays in Stratford-upon-Avon, where the guests ponder whether to use or not to use the self-catering facilities. After some Shakespearean dress-up in the afternoon, two guests compare their nights sleep to kipping in a rave, leaving the hosts with a tragedy at feedback time

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Karen and Adrian stake a claim for being the busiest BandBers in Four in a Bed history at The Barn in North Yorkshire - a BandB, village shop and post office all rolled into one. But one guest reckons that such a big to-do list means the hosts havent paid enough attention to his poached eggs at breakfast

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The weeks final visit is to Dilham Hall Retreats in Norfolk, hosted by farming couple Louise and Luke. Their riverside pods take everyones breath away, and a boat ride on the Norfolk Broads leaves them all feeling calm and serene - until attention turns to the looming drama of payment day

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Its been a week of tired eyes and undesired flies as the BandB owners take it in turns as hosts to decide which of their establishments provides the best value for money. On Payment Day, the weeks scores are revealed and the winner announced

programme.title: The Emergency Ward
programme.desc: Doctors Emma and Jonathan fear that a truck driver is bleeding internally from pelvic injuries after a high-speed crash. Dr Zaheer and nurse Ebony are vigilant for heart failure in a 28-year-old woman whos having a severe asthma attack and struggling to breathe despite multiple medications, while Dr Scott expects the worst when a 74-year-old man, who already has lung disease, is pinned under the car he was working on at home

programme.title: 24 Hours in AandE
programme.desc: Eighty-one-year-old cyclist Bill is brought to St Georges from Surrey after being hit by a car. He was not wearing a helmet, and doctors are concerned he may have sustained a serious head injury. Meanwhile, 93-year-old Amy arrives in AandE with her friend after a clothes airer fell on top of her while she was hanging out the washing. Plus, an emergency nurse practitioner looks after 78-year-old Samuel, who landed on his face after falling over some boxes

programme.title: 24 Hours in AandE
programme.desc: Ten-year-old Ashleigh is rushed to St Georges by ambulance after twisting her leg while playing football. X-rays reveal that her femur is broken and she will need surgery. Meanwhile, three-year-old Lola visits AandE with her dad after having a seizure, and 91-year-old Betty arrives with severe chest pain. Doctors carry out a blood test in order to find out whether she is having a heart attack and will require life-saving surgery

programme.title: The Emergency Ward
programme.desc: Doctors Emma and Jonathan fear that a truck driver is bleeding internally from pelvic injuries after a high-speed crash. Dr Zaheer and nurse Ebony are vigilant for heart failure in a 28-year-old woman whos having a severe asthma attack and struggling to breathe despite multiple medications, while Dr Scott expects the worst when a 74-year-old man, who already has lung disease, is pinned under the car he was working on at home

programme.title: 24 Hours in AandE
programme.desc: Eighty-one-year-old cyclist Bill is brought to St Georges from Surrey after being hit by a car. He was not wearing a helmet, and doctors are concerned he may have sustained a serious head injury. Meanwhile, 93-year-old Amy arrives in AandE with her friend after a clothes airer fell on top of her while she was hanging out the washing. Plus, an emergency nurse practitioner looks after 78-year-old Samuel, who landed on his face after falling over some boxes

programme.title: 24 Hours in AandE
programme.desc: Train driver Andy, 48, is rushed in after falling 12ft from a tree while cutting branches and landing on his feet, badly fracturing both ankles, and doctors are concerned the impact may have caused life-changing damage to critical nerves and blood vessels in his feet and legs. Angela, 59, has come to St Georges as her heart has been beating at over 150bpm for eight hours, risking stroke and other complications. Cardiologist Dr Aruns attempts to bring down her pulse using a powerful drug arent successful, so he decides to electrically shock her heart to reset it

programme.title: Food Unwrapped
programme.desc: Jimmy Doherty and Briony May Williams embark on an epic journey through the Nordic region. Starting in the Viking fjords of west Norway, they take to the seas to uncover how cod farming could be part of the future. Travelling south through Swedens ancient landscape, they pay a special visit to a certain furniture store and learn just what makes a meatball so Swedish. They finish their journey in Finland, home to the happiest people in the world apparently, to find out what kind of food is putting a smile on their faces

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon OBrien come across a gramophone and a stow-away bed to restore. Meanwhile, Simon accidently puts his foot through a drum

programme.title: Emergency Helicopter Medics
programme.desc: In Oxfordshire, cyclist Matt suffers life-threatening injuries after a collision with a car. On the A1, 64-year-old John is trapped in his car after a head-on collision. Meanwhile, in Cumbria, heli-medics fly to one of the most remote roads in Britain to help Caroline who has come off a motorbike and is injured

programme.title: Emergency Helicopter Medics
programme.desc: Heli-medics race to a skydiver whos suffered serious injuries after crashing into a house. Theres a race across the country to reach a man who has fallen 40ft from a ladder. Plus, in Berkshire, biker Hanna is trapped with multiple injuries after hitting a road sign

programme.title: 24 Hours in AandE
programme.desc: Three-year-old Louie is airlifted to St Georges after being run over by a car while playing outside, with doctors expressing concern about his neck, which seems to be stuck in one position. Elsewhere, 14-year-old Josh is treated after injuring his ankle when riding a BMX with his father on the front. Meanwhile, 41-year-old Sam arrives in resus with a cut to her head and possible spinal and internal injuries after losing control of her car and crashing into a tree

programme.title: Food Unwrapped
programme.desc: Kate Quilton heads to Kenya to see how cold-brew coffee differs from iced coffee. Helen Lawal tries frozen pastries in Portugal to see how well they perform well against fresh, while Briony May Williams heads to Staffordshire to learn how parsnips are cultivated to taste so sweet

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The competition heads to Anglesey, where best pals Richard Tuke and Susan Hatch hope to show that Blackthorn Farm BandB, which they own, is better than any of their guests establishments. However, they find that their close friendship is scrutinised even more than their property

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The White Swan Hotel in Yeadon, West Yorkshire, is the second stop of the competition, and owners Graham and Barbara Yellow hope their basic facilities and affordable prices will please their guests. Spiritual couple Malcolm and Fiona Piers-Taylor get good vibes from their room, Rachel Lonergan views the hosts in a new light and breakfast is well received by the contestants, despite having to pay a supplement fee

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Vegetarians Malcolm and Fiona Piers-Taylor host at the Old Mill House BandB in Bridport, Dorset, and both come under attack at dinner when Malcolm voices his opinions on meat eating. In the morning, a meditation session gets a mixed response, and most of the guests are left cold by the vegetarian buffet breakfast

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Rachel and Dan Lonergan welcome their guests to the Brighton Inn, East Sussex, where they hope to offer a luxury, boutique experience. Malcolm and Fiona Piers-Taylor give their room a thorough inspection, before the groups learns the art of building a human pyramid in the afternoons activity. At breakfast, they discover a lack of vegetarian alternatives, and the hosts suspect game-playing when they are shocked by some negative feedback

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The BandB owners come together to confront their rivals and find out how much they have been paid. Richard and Susan are keen to tackle the bathroom issues of Barbara and Graham, who bask in the comments from Malcolm and Fiona. The Old Mill House proprietors defend their vegetarian breakfast routine, but serve up snobby feedback to Rachel and Dan from the Brighton Inn

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Leah Charles-King comes to the help of Kerry and Stewart from Newcastle who, with five children and eight grandchildren, are looking forward to finding a little oasis on the Canary island of Lanzarote that they can all escape to. Leah lines up five properties for thepair to consider within their budget of £120,000

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Danni Menzies comes to the aid of husband and wife Paul and Tracy, who are hunting around the villages of Lake Vinuela in southern Spain for their dream holiday home

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Kevin McCloud meets actor Sean Simons, who has bought the ruins of Cloontykilla Castle in Co Roscommon with a view to using them to create a spectacular mock 16th-century home. However, the outlandish plans - including Jacuzzis in the battlements and water-spouting gargoyles - have Kevin worried the project could become a garish nightmare, and his scepticism increases when he realises Sean has not consulted an architect. Disaster seems to be looming as the contractors grow frustrated and planning regulations are ignored, but somehow things slowly begin to take shape - until a national economic crisis plunges everything into chaos

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Kevin McCloud meets artists Indi Waterstone and her partner Rebecca, who are planning to construct a modern, larch-clad home on the Isle of Skye, with a second hand-crafted building alongside to use as their studio, all on a tight budget of £150,000. They have been saving for years, but making it happen will mean taking on the savage weather, not to mention the sceptical locals - and as if that werent enough, most of the work is being done by one man, local builder Donald

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Oil and gas co-ordinator Danielle Greenwood is keen to impress as she hosts the first dinner party in the Merseyside area, as she welcomes divorcee Jade Chantelle Short, Michael Buble impersonator Jamie Somers, pastoral care manager Steven Booth, and nursery school teacher Anna Senior into her home. Danielle takes no risks with a down-to-earth menu, which she hopes will help bag her the £1,000 prize at the end of the week

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Pastoral care manager Steven Booth hosts the second Merseyside dinner party, and names each course after a Tina Turner song, hoping to win the £1,000 prize. The food is well received, but tensions rise when one guest makes a tactless comment

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Glamorous PR representative Jade Chantelle hosts the third night in Merseyside, where she hopes a casino-themed evening with lashings of champagne and a high-class menu will help her to win the £1,000 prize

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Primary school teacher Anna Senior hosts the fourth night in Merseyside with a fairy tale-themed evening. Her menu consists of a Mad Hatters soup and sandwich, a special pork dish cooked three ways and baked cheesecake for dessert, but she must try to control the tension between Jamie and Jade to have a successful party

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Michael Buble impersonator Jamie Somers hosts the final Merseyside dinner party, but may have to put aside his differences with guest Jade if he is to be crowned the winner of the £1,000 cash prize

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Inexperienced father-and-daughter team Steve and Elise host the weeks first visit, at 35 Main Street in Pembrokeshire, offering guests a visit to a local brewery

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: For the weeks second visit, Anjana and David are aiming to get their guests to branch out at Chillderness, their unique off-grid site in Powys, complete with tree-top accommodation. Alas, the outdoor toilets mean some of the BandBers are far from keen to lay down roots. And although everyone has a hoot on a trip to meet some birds of prey, the self-catering breakfast leads to some unsavoury feedback

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: At Redberth Gardens in Tenby, hosts Carys and Rhys get off to a flyer thanks to the facilities at their glampsite, which include hot tubs, cinema screens and outdoor table football. But tensions soon emerge during a trip to the zoo, as two BandBers cant help but revisit past comments

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The final visit is to Tanya and Garys Bae Abermaw BandB, in Barmouth, Gwynedd. Theyre hoping their incredible views will put them in sight of the winners plaque - but its the vista looking into the room rather than out that doesnt delight one set of guests. Can an impressive display at breakfast turn things around?

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: After an eventful week in Wales, the BandBers meet up once more to debate their differences and learn who has won. Will some lingering vendettas spoil the party?

programme.title: Chernobyl: Inside the Meltdown
programme.desc: Survivors recount the terrifying first 24-hours of the worst nuclear accident in history. After a reactor explodes during a safety test at a power plant in Ukraine, firefighters rush to the scene, unaware of the lethal levels of radiation. Soviet authorities try to cover up the disaster as a cloud of radiation begins to circle the world

programme.title: Chernobyl: Inside the Meltdown
programme.desc: Two days after the explosion, the world wakes up to a terrifying global disaster. At the power plant, helicopter pilots try to put out a fire in the reactor core, braving plumes of highly radioactive smoke. Soviet authorities eventually begin to evacuate 50,000 people from the local area, while still trying to conceal the scale of the accident

programme.title: Chernobyl: Inside the Meltdown
programme.desc: Workers begin to clean-up the deadly radioactive fallout from the explosion. After troops establish an exclusion zone, Gorbachev orders the construction of a vast concrete sarcophagus to cover the destroyed nuclear reactor. In Vienna, the world finally hears the Soviet explanation of what went wrong - but are they telling the truth?

programme.title: Chernobyl: Inside the Meltdown
programme.desc: 14 months after the disaster, plant operators face a Soviet show trial. At the reactor, scientists hunt for the deadly remains of the radioactive fuel. As the USSR crumbles, an independent investigation reveals the true cause of the accident, and the full scale of the cover up. 40 years later, the nuclear exclusion zone turns into a war zone

programme.title: Car S.O.S.

programme.title: Construction Fails
programme.desc: Brace for disaster as highways fall, bridges collapse and buildings crumble in a global tour of shocking engineering failures

programme.title: Drain the Oceans
programme.desc: Scientific data and digital re-creations are used to explore the wreckage of ships from the Battle of Jutland in 1916 to try to answer a question that has gone unanswered for more than 100 years - which side actually won. The programme also examines the wrecks at the bottom of the Scapa Flow, where the German fleet was scuttled in 1919

programme.title: Eyewitness WWII: Invasion Italy
programme.desc: The story of the Second World Wars Italian campaign told through the accounts of those who fought in it, beginning as Allied troops faced the Axis in Sicily, Salerno and Anzio

programme.title: Eyewitness WWII: Invasion Italy
programme.desc: Part two of two. The story of the Second World Wars Italian campaign told through the accounts of those who fought in it

programme.title: Air Crash Investigation
programme.desc: Reconstruction of a 2010 disaster, when the pilots of Qantas Flight 32 were forced to deal with a series of failures resulting from an explosion in the engine of their A380

programme.title: Air Crash Investigation
programme.desc: ACI examines one of the greatest aviation mysteries: the missing Malaysia Airlines Flight 370

programme.title: Seconds from Disaster
programme.desc: A look at the strategy and series of errors that led to the sinking of the Bismarck in 1941, an incident during which more than 2,000 German sailors were killed

programme.title: Chernobyl: Inside the Meltdown
programme.desc: Survivors recount the terrifying first 24-hours of the worst nuclear accident in history. After a reactor explodes during a safety test at a power plant in Ukraine, firefighters rush to the scene, unaware of the lethal levels of radiation. Soviet authorities try to cover up the disaster as a cloud of radiation begins to circle the world

programme.title: Chernobyl: Inside the Meltdown
programme.desc: Two days after the explosion, the world wakes up to a terrifying global disaster. At the power plant, helicopter pilots try to put out a fire in the reactor core, braving plumes of highly radioactive smoke. Soviet authorities eventually begin to evacuate 50,000 people from the local area, while still trying to conceal the scale of the accident

programme.title: Chernobyl: Inside the Meltdown
programme.desc: Workers begin to clean-up the deadly radioactive fallout from the explosion. After troops establish an exclusion zone, Gorbachev orders the construction of a vast concrete sarcophagus to cover the destroyed nuclear reactor. In Vienna, the world finally hears the Soviet explanation of what went wrong - but are they telling the truth?

programme.title: Chernobyl: Inside the Meltdown
programme.desc: 14 months after the disaster, plant operators face a Soviet show trial. At the reactor, scientists hunt for the deadly remains of the radioactive fuel. As the USSR crumbles, an independent investigation reveals the true cause of the accident, and the full scale of the cover up. 40 years later, the nuclear exclusion zone turns into a war zone

programme.title: Ukraine: War from the Air
programme.desc: A year after Russias invasion, this documentary charts the heroic efforts of Ukraines people to defeat Russias army in a war fought and observed from the air

programme.title: Construction Fails

programme.title: Plane Crash Recreated
programme.desc: The events of Febtuary 25, 2009, when Turkish Airlines Flight 1951 went down less than a mile from the runway at Amsterdam Schiphol Airport, breaking into three pieces on impact and killing nine passengers and crew and all three pilots. A faulty radio altimeter was the initial catalyst in a chain of errors that led to the horrific crash. Here, survivors tell their story of escaping from the wreckage of what should have been a normal commute home to Amsterdam

programme.title: Franco: The Last Inquisitor
programme.sub-title: In the Name of Christ and the Empire
programme.desc: Part one of two. The life of Spanish general and dictator Francisco Franco Bahamonde, who experienced a remarkable rise through the ranks of the Spanish army until the Republican regime, in 1931, brought his career to a halt. The Spanish Civil War became his opportunity to seize absolute power in Spain.

programme.title: Franco: The Last Inquisitor
programme.desc: Part two of two. The life of Spanish general and dictator Francisco Franco Bahamonde. Having come to power through a coup, General Franco maneuvered from 1939 until his death in 1975 to remain in control. While his former allies Hitler and Mussolini disappeared with the end of the Second World War, his political longevity remains surprising to this day

programme.title: The Siege of Leningrad
programme.desc: The 872-day German and Finnish encirclement of the Soviet city between September 1941 and January 1944, which saw its inhabitants falling into despair, starvation and cannibalism. This documentary features extensive interviews with historian Anna Reid, while eyewitnesses recall the dramatic events. Original diaries and documents, along with rarely seen film and photographic material, illustrate the tragedy

programme.title: Weathered: Earths Extremes
programme.sub-title: Are We at the Tipping Point?
programme.desc: Maiya May journeys across the United States to explore climate tipping points. Discovering how critical climate systems are for the planet?s stability, and learning the science behind these changes

programme.title: Hitlers Gold
programme.desc: In Munich, in September 1919, history was about to be changed by a penniless army corporal thirsty to avenge his adoptive motherland. Thirteen years later, Adolf Hitler would be saluted as the saviour of the German nation. This episode examines the covert financial web that Hitler spun across Europe and around the globe to bankroll his dream of world domination

programme.title: Hitlers Gold
programme.desc: In 1937, German leader Adolf Hitler appeared to be fulfilling his promise of returning his nation to prosperity. However, there was a deeply sinister side to the recovery. This episodes focuses on his efforts to bankroll his dream of world domination

programme.title: Hitlers Gold
programme.desc: The series exploring the covert financial web spun by the Nazis continues. This episode explores how the Nazis finances were affected by preparing for and fighting war in Europe

programme.title: Hitlers Gold
programme.desc: As the Second World War ended, the hunt for the Nazi criminals began. Amid a battle for justice, there was also the search for the property the Nazis had stolen

programme.title: Chernobyl: The New Evidence
programme.desc: Part one of two. Documentary exploring catalogue of errors that occurred in the run-up to the nuclear disaster, examining newly declassified evidence from the KGB archives about the safety of the plant. The programme examines concerns that had been voiced while the nuclear plant was still under construction, but which were ignored by the Soviet leaders

programme.title: Chernobyl: The New Evidence
programme.desc: Using previously classified KGB documents, the second part of this film explores how far Soviet leaders were willing to go to cover up the Chernobyl nuclear disaster of 1986, including exposing children to toxic fallout at the Kyiv May Day parade to prove to the world that all was fine. Featuring first-hand accounts from those who survived the blast, including Oleksiy Ananenko, who braved radioactive waters to prevent a deadlier second explosion and Maryna Sivets, whose unborn childs life was put at risk

programme.title: The Champions

programme.title: Sing 2 (2021)
programme.desc: With his theatrical company a local success, Buster Moon is dreaming of bigger things. Unfortunately, when a talent scout dismisses their work as inadequate for the big time, Buster is driven to prove her wrong. Buster inspires his players to gamble everything to sneak into a talent audition for the demanding entertainment mogul Jimmy Crystal. Against the odds, they catch his interest with some frantic creative improvisation and even more desperate lies like personally knowing the reclusive rock star Clay Calloway, who has not been seen in 15 years. Animated musical sequel, with the voices of Matthew McConaughey, Reese Witherspoon, Scarlett Johansson and Bono

programme.title: Super Garden
programme.desc: Five amateur designers take over a garden in a new housing estate - with the winning entry being recreated at Bord Bia Bloom, Irelands premier garden show. First up is Eoighan Mountaine Barry from Lucan in Dublin, whose striking design mixes hard materials like glass bricks with natural elements, such as his olive tree centrepiece. Judging his efforts are Kerrie Gardener, Carol Marks, Monica Alvarez and Brian Burke - a previous Super Garden winner

programme.title: Live UEFA Womens Champions League
programme.desc: Arsenal v Lyon (Kick-off 3.30pm). Marie Crowe presents all the action from the first leg of the semi-final, held at Emirates Stadium. Arsenal edged past London rivals Chelsea 3-2 on aggregate in the quarter-finals to maintain their chances of retaining the trophy. Lyon are record eight-time champions of Europe and scored three goals in extra time in the last eight to overcome VfL Wolfsburg 4-0 on the night and 4-1 over the two legs

programme.title: Bridge of Lies
programme.desc: Family members Suraj, Nisha, Tina and Rob compete for money by crossing a bridge of stepping stones across the studio floor. Quiz show, hosted by Ross Kemp

programme.title: Grand Designs: House of the Year
programme.desc: Kevin McCloud visits five recently transformed homes that have been shortlisted for the RIBA House of the Year award. A barn renovation in Sussex, a reborn parchment factory in Northamptonshire, an eco-friendly mews house in London, a former cowshed in Dorset and two fused flats in the Kyles of Bute

programme.title: Grand Designs: House of the Year
programme.desc: Kevin McCloud visits homes that, along with being nominated for the RIBA House of the Year prize, have pioneered a new architectural style by fusing mock-Tudor, modernism and industrial chic. A home in Derbyshire that experiments with reorganising space, a small-space design in an infill plot, an eco-extension on a historic cottage in Suffolk and a home in London remodelled complete with a mountain on the roof

programme.title: UCL Magazine Show
programme.desc: Including reviews of recent Champions League matches, a round-up of the latest news, plus interviews with leading players and coaches

programme.title: Amazing Hotels: Life Beyond the Lobby
programme.desc: Rob Rinder and Monica Galetti travel to Turkey to work at Argos in Cappadocia, a hotel built into a network of ancient caves. Combining history with modern luxury, Monica finds herself brushing cave ceilings with housekeeping before flying above in a hot air balloon, while Rob helps with the finishing touches of a new spa. With each room providing surprises and inviting curiosity, Rob seeks further education on the history of the caves as Monica practises centuries-old traditional cooking techniques

programme.title: Chernobyl: The New Evidence
programme.desc: Part one of two. Documentary exploring catalogue of errors that occurred in the run-up to the nuclear disaster, examining newly declassified evidence from the KGB archives about the safety of the plant. The programme examines concerns that had been voiced while the nuclear plant was still under construction, but which were ignored by the Soviet leaders

programme.title: Chernobyl: The New Evidence
programme.desc: Using previously classified KGB documents, the second part of this film explores how far Soviet leaders were willing to go to cover up the Chernobyl nuclear disaster of 1986, including exposing children to toxic fallout at the Kyiv May Day parade to prove to the world that all was fine. Featuring first-hand accounts from those who survived the blast, including Oleksiy Ananenko, who braved radioactive waters to prevent a deadlier second explosion and Maryna Sivets, whose unborn childs life was put at risk

programme.title: Mammoth
programme.desc: Tony turns into a protective father figure when Mel goes on a date with new man Matthew, but he quickly starts trying to develop the worlds strangest bromance. He also takes his self-appointed role as head of PE very seriously, until the pressure gets too much, while Theo revels in his new bad-boy image

programme.title: The Saturday Game
programme.desc: Damian Lawlor is joined by Conor McManus and Ursula Jacob for highlights and analysis of the days Championship action. programme.title: The Responder
programme.desc: Chris has to stay ahead of Franny whilst deciding what to do with his phone. As he wavers, Franny hunts him throughout the city. Drama, starring Martin Freeman

programme.title: The Traitors
programme.desc: As the players reel from the revelations at the round table, in the dead of the night the traitors plot their next move. As a new day beckons, £10,000 is at stake as the players face one of their toughest missions yet, leaving them fired up for the next round table. A brutal banishment leaves one player in tears, and as the castle reels from the events of the evening, the Traitors must decide which faithful to set their sights on next. Presented by Claudia Winkleman

programme.title: Secrets and Spies: A Nuclear Game
programme.desc: Documenting the tensions between East and West, which led to a spying game and an arms race that brought the world to the brink of nuclear war. Plus, a look at the unpublished letters of Michael Bettaney and interviews with former aides to Margaret Thatcher and Ronald Reagan

programme.desc: Documentary following the Donatella Flick Conducting Competition, in which 20 young conductors from across Europe compete for the chance to become an LSO assistant conductor

programme.title: The Shadows: The Final Tour
programme.desc: A 2004 concert by the instrumental rock band at the Cardiff Indoor Arena, in which Hank Marvin, Bruce Welch and Brian Bennett reunited to perform a selection of their greatest hits. Including performances of Apache, Riders in the Sky, Dont Cry for Me Argentina, Theme from The Deer Hunter and FBI

programme.title: How the Beatles Changed the World
programme.desc: Through exclusive interviews and a wealth of rarely seen footage, this film reveals the previously untold story of how four young men from Liverpool transformed the world for ever

programme.title: Stuart Sutcliffe: The Lost Beatle
programme.desc: Profile of the less well-known member of the group. Sutcliffe met John Lennon at art college, and played bass on their early tours of Scotland and Germany. However, he left the band to study art in Hamburg, and ultimately died of a brain haemorrhage before they became famous. Part of the Liverpool on the Box week

programme.title: Sam Smith: Love Goes - Live at Abbey Road Studios
programme.desc: A 2020 concert by the singer-songwriter, featuring performances of Diamonds, Promises, Kids Again, Dancing with a Stranger. Lay Me Down and Stay With Me

programme.title: The Doors Live at the Hollywood Bowl 68
programme.desc: Footage of a concert by the band staged at the Los Angeles amphitheatre on July 4, 1968, including performances of Alabama Song, Light My Fire and The End

programme.title: Discovering Music
programme.desc: A look at the life and career of the American folk-rock group, whose easygoing vocal harmonies provided the soundtrack to the late 1960s

programme.title: Discovering Music
programme.desc: Music critics look at the life and career of James Brown, who was dubbed the Godfather of Soul and was one of the most influential musicians of all time

programme.title: Don Giovanni Paris Opera
programme.desc: The Paris Opera opens its 2023-24 season with Opera Bastilles production of Don Giovanni. Since its Prague premiere in 1787, Mozarts opera score achieves an unparalleled convergence of comedy and drama detailing the myth of Don Giovanni, the philanderer who defies God and society

programme.title: Tales of the Unexpected
programme.desc: A man with a remarkable memory plans to turn his talent into cash by offering to help someone recall a locker number. Starring Bernard Cribbins, Colin Blakely and Judy Geeson

programme.title: Tales of the Unexpected
programme.desc: A near-bankrupt hotel owner goes to great lengths to get her hands on an inheritance from a rich aunt. Starring Hayley Mills, Stuart Wilson and Phyllida Law

programme.title: Tales of the Unexpected
programme.desc: A man has an unpleasant shock when he learns his recently deceased father owed a large amount of money. Starring Evelyn Laye, Richard Pearson, Hugh Fraser and David Webb

programme.title: Tales of the Unexpected
programme.desc: A sheriff investigates a murder, but encounters problems when it turns out the main suspect has an identical twin. Starring David Cassidy and Darren McGavin

programme.title: The Alfred Hitchcock Hour
programme.desc: When a mans boyhood friends escape from prison, he and his wifes perfect suburban world is interrupted as a past crime returns to haunt him. With Hugh OBrian and Gena Rowlands

programme.title: The Alfred Hitchcock Hour
programme.desc: Remembering nothing of the day before, an alcoholic painfully reconstructs the events of what proves to have been a catastrophic day

programme.title: Making a Maestro
programme.desc: Documentary following the Donatella Flick Conducting Competition, in which 20 young conductors from across Europe compete for the chance to become an LSO assistant conductor

programme.title: Alec Guinness: A Class Act
programme.desc: A look at the career of the actor through the words of his family and friends who knew and worked with him, as well as archive interviews with Guinness and voice recordings from his autobiography

programme.title: The Ventures: Stars on Guitars
programme.desc: Documentary telling the story of the instrumental rock group, who emerged in the late-1950s and helped popularise the electric guitar across the world during the 1960s

programme.title: Johnny Cash - A Night to Remember Live 1973
programme.desc: A performance by the country star at Los Angeles Ahmanson Theatre, with songs including Big River, Jackson, Folsom Prison Blues, A Boy Named Sue and Sunday Morning Coming Down

programme.title: Spymasters: The Great Spy Writers
programme.desc: A look at the work of Len Deighton and Mick Herron, as well as a reflection on recent intelligence history, from the defection of Oleg Gordievsky to the killing of Osama bin Laden

programme.title: The Stranger (1946)
programme.desc: A Nazi war criminal masquerades as a college professor in an American town, while a government official is intent on tracking him down. Drama, directed by and starring Orson Welles, with Loretta Young and Edward G Robinson

programme.title: Roy Orbison Forever
programme.desc: Documentary celebrating the singer-songwriter, charting his career and relationships with other musicians through interviews and archive performances. Featuring interviews with Elvis Costello, Tom Petty, Bono, Gerry Marsden, Jeff Lynne, Olivia Harrison and Barbara Orbison

programme.title: Opry Live
programme.desc: A selection of performances from world-famous Nashville music venue the Grand Ole Opry, featuring Keith Urban, Scotty McCreery and 49 Winchester

programme.title: Jamie Cullum Live at Blenheim Palace
programme.desc: A 2004 concert at the Oxfordshire stately home by the jazz musician and his band, featuring songs by Cole Porter, Jimi Hendrix, Alan Jay Lerner, Radiohead and N.E.R.D

programme.title: Seal Plays Baloise Session
programme.desc: The singer performs at the Swiss indoor festival in 2024, featuring a selection of jazz standards as well as hits including Killer, Kiss from a Rose and Crazy

programme.title: Eddy Grant: Live in London
programme.desc: A 1986 concert by the musician at the South Banks Festival Gardens

programme.title: Discovering Music
programme.desc: Music journalists examine the life and career of the musician, whose hits include You Are the Sunshine of My Life and Superstition

programme.title: Discovering Music
programme.desc: Music journalists examine the life and music of the American singer-songwriter, whose hits included Oh, Pretty Woman, Only the Lonely and Crying

programme.title: A Midsummer Nights Dream
programme.desc: John Neumeiers production of the ballet based on Shakespeares play, weaving a tangled web of love, jealousy and magic as fairies, mortals and rustics collide in a dreamlike world

programme.title: Artist of the Year: Masterclass
programme.desc: Tutorials for budding artists, with each edition focusing on a different subject or technique, while Tai-Shan Schierenberg has tips on painting portraits

programme.title: Artist of the Year: Masterclass
programme.desc: Tutorials by painters from both Portrait and Landscape Artist of the Year series, helping viewers understand the basics of painting and drawing

programme.title: Tales of the Unexpected
programme.desc: A man with a remarkable memory plans to turn his talent into cash by offering to help someone recall a locker number. Starring Bernard Cribbins, Colin Blakely and Judy Geeson

programme.title: Tales of the Unexpected
programme.desc: A near-bankrupt hotel owner goes to great lengths to get her hands on an inheritance from a rich aunt. Starring Hayley Mills, Stuart Wilson and Phyllida Law

programme.title: Alfred Hitchcock Presents
programme.desc: A struggling deputy DA gets a murder conviction against a man for killing his wife, but is confronted by another who claims he committed the crime. With Pat Hingle and Georgann Johnson

programme.title: Alfred Hitchcock Presents
programme.desc: Eddie Slovak meets an old Army buddy named Pete who once saved his life. He intends to repay his friend. He offers him money for a business, but Peter refuses. Eddie tries to talk to Petes wife Louise. Louise falls for Eddie and the two become romantically involved. With Alex Nicol and Nita Talbot

programme.title: The Last of Us
programme.desc: Five years after the events in Salt Lake City, a now 19-year-old Ellie makes a discovery while on patrol with her best friend Dina. Back in Jackson Hole, Joel seeks help to mend his relationship with Ellie. The return of the post-apocalyptic drama, starring Pedro Pascal and Bella Ramsey

programme.title: Homicide: Life on the Street
programme.desc: Bayliss goes it alone to investigate the murder of a cop shooter when his colleagues refuse to help him. Gritty police drama, starring Daniel Baldwin and Kyle Secor

programme.title: Homicide: Life on the Street
programme.desc: Howard returns to work, still feeling weak after the shooting, but solves a murder case from her desk to prove her mettle. Gritty police drama, starring Melissa Leo

programme.title: Urban Secrets
programme.desc: Alan Cumming visits Liverpool, cutting through cliches about football and the Beatles to explore its less-celebrated treasures. Among his destinations are a 1930s banking building that has remained unaltered since closing in 2007, a well-appointed set of toilets and a recording studio hidden in a bar. He also discovers which former dictator once spent Christmas in the city

programme.title: Urban Secrets
programme.desc: Alan Cumming discovers more little-known facts on a journey between Smithfields and Spitalfields, two of Londons most famous markets. He begins with a fry-up breakfast before visiting the remains of a Roman amphitheatre and investigating a mysterious secret in a Liverpool Street hotel, as well as meeting former EastEnders star Larry Lamb (Archie Mitchell)

programme.title: Richard E Grants Hotel Secrets
programme.desc: The actor returns to explore more of the worlds most luxurious hotels and begins in Miamis South Beach, home to some of the most hedonistic and extravagant establishments in America. At the Fontainebleau, he meets a man with eye-opening stories about Frank Sinatras regular visits and gets a shock when he discovers how much it costs to hire a booth in the night club. Richard also gatecrashes swimwear fashion week at the Raleigh Hotel, where he speaks to the event manager about the many celebrity parties he has organised

programme.title: Richard E Grants Hotel Secrets
programme.desc: In this edition the actor explores the luxury hotels of Hong Kong. Home to more skyscrapers than any other city, he first checks in to the Mandarin in the financial district, before experiencing the Four Seasons, the only hotel with two Michelin three-star resturants, as well as the timeless Peninsula in Kowloon, which even provides a Rolls-Royce airport taxi service. He ends his stay at the Ritz-Carlton, home to the worlds highest swimming pool

programme.title: Deadwood

programme.title: Deadwood
programme.desc: Wolcott continues to make enemies for himself in the town, leading the camps leaders to call a meeting after a public brawl with Utter. programme.title: Deadwood

programme.title: Homicide: Life on the Street
programme.desc: Pembleton arrests the prime suspect in the shooting of his three fellow law-enforcers. Gritty police drama, starring Daniel Baldwin and Andre Braugher

programme.title: True Detective
programme.desc: As Prior digs into the links between Tsalal and mining company Silver Sky, Navarro rescues Leah from a local protest that has turned violent. Later, Hank receives a quid pro quo offer

programme.title: Homicide: Life on the Street
programme.desc: Pembleton helps to cover up a scandal, only to find it coming back to haunt him. Gritty police drama, starring Andre Braugher, Daniel Baldwin and Richard Belzer

programme.title: Homicide: Life on the Street
programme.desc: Russert learns one of his former partners is being transferred back into Homicide. Pembleton chooses not to implicate the commissioner in the case that cost him his job. Gritty police drama, starring Daniel Baldwin, Ned Beatty and Richard Belzer

programme.title: Homicide: Life on the Street
programme.desc: Part one of three. Howard, Felton and Bolander are ambushed and shot as they try to arrest a suspect. Gritty police drama, starring Daniel Baldwin, Ned Beatty and Richard Belzer

programme.title: Sort Of
programme.desc: After shocking news, Sabi takes some time to process the situation - but their absence soon causes loved ones to worry

programme.title: Urban Secrets
programme.desc: Alan Cumming explores Newcastle, discovering strange sounds by the river and finding out how traditional culture is being reinterpreted in the Byker district. He goes behind the scenes at Newcastle United with local football hero Peter Beardsley, learns about the history of adult comic Viz from former editor Simon Donald and accompanies comedienne Sarah Millican to her favourite part of the city

programme.title: Richard E Grants Hotel Secrets
programme.desc: The actor explores Berlin, visiting the arty Propeller Island City Lodge - a hotel where guests can fulfil their fantasies by sleeping in lion cages, coffins or rotating beds. At the Schlosshotel in affluent Grunewald, cabaret dancer Romy Haag talks to Richard about her affair with David Bowie, who stayed at the hotel while living in the German capital in the 1970s

programme.title: Richard E Grants Hotel Secrets
programme.desc: The actor heads to New Orleans and meets soul singer Irma Thomas at the Hotel de Monteleone, an establishment that was popular with writers such as Tennessee Williams, F Scott Fitzgerald and Ernest Hemingway. Richard speaks to Steel Magnolias author Robert Harling about the Soniat House, check into the supposedly haunted Hotel Maison de Ville and talks to burlesque dancer Chrissy Owens at the Roosevelt

programme.title: Deadwood
programme.desc: Swearengen strikes a departure deal with Miss Isringhausen and orders Farnum to befriend Blasanov so he can monitor any telegrams coming in from Yankton. Bullock gains possession of a safe full of money, while Hostetler and the General acquire a valuable wild horse they hope to sell to the cavalry. Drama, starring Ian McShane and Timothy Olyphant

programme.title: Deadwood
programme.desc: The town is left reeling in the wake of Williams accident, and Hostetler and the General flee in fear of retribution once word gets out that the horse was in their care. Meanwhile, Swearengen finally receives word from Yankton. Drama, starring Ian McShane, Brad Dourif and Timothy Olyphant

programme.title: Deadwood
programme.desc: The residents of Deadwood face a sad day when Williams funeral is held, and Seth Bullock and his wife prepare to leave the town following their sons tragic death. Meanwhile, Alma offers her answer to Ellsworth. Timothy Olyphant and Anna Gunn star

programme.title: Deadwood
programme.desc: A nervous Alma and Ellsworth get married, and Tolliver informs George Hearst of Wolcotts murderous acts. When the engineer is confronted, he takes drastic action with devastating consequences. Wu launches an attack on Lee

programme.title: Deadwood
programme.sub-title: Tell Your God to Ready for Blood
programme.desc: Seth Bullock and bartender Harry Manning compete for the office of sheriff, and EB Farnum and Sol Star campaign to become mayor. Meanwhile, Swearengen continues to view George Hearst with suspicion. Western drama set in a lawless US town, starring Ian McShane

programme.title: Deadwood
programme.sub-title: I Am Not the Fine Man You Take Me For
programme.desc: While competition for the roles of sheriff and mayor is hotting up, Jane achieves a captive audience the candidates can only dream of. Swearengen receives a cryptic note informing him of another bloody incident at the Gem, but Hurst later makes his intentions known in a more straightforward message. Meanwhile, Andy Cramed pays an unexpected visit to Tolliver. Starring Ian McShane and Robin Weigert

programme.title: Deadwood
programme.desc: Emmy award-winner Brian Cox joins the cast as a flamboyant stage promoter on a mission to introduce a modicum of culture to the residents of the gold-rush town. Hearsts long-serving cook also makes an appearance as the Grand Central chef, and Wu returns with news of a soon-to-arrive group of imported workers. Meanwhile, Bullock uncovers the truth about the Gem killings. Cleo King guest stars

programme.title: Chernobyl
programme.desc: In April 1986, workers and firefighters put their lives on the line to try to deal with an explosion at the Chernobyl nuclear power plant near the city of Pripyat in Ukraine. Fact-based drama, starring Jared Harris, Emily Watson and Stellan Skarsgard

programme.title: Chernobyl
programme.desc: With millions of lives already at risk, Ulana races to warn leading Soviet nuclear physicist Valery Legasov about the threat of second, devastating explosion

programme.title: Chernobyl
programme.desc: Valery Legasov draws up a plan, but it comes with great risks. Meanwhile, Lyudmilla ignores warnings about her firefighter husbands contamination

programme.title: Chernobyl
programme.desc: Valery Legasov and Boris Shcherbina come up with a life-saving idea to remove radioactive debris. Elsewhere, Ulana faces problems as she investigates the explosion

programme.title: Chernobyl
programme.desc: Valery Legasov, Boris Shcherbina and Ulana Khomyuk risk their lives and their reputations to expose the truth about the Chernobyl disaster

programme.title: All Her Fault
programme.desc: Reeling from the home invasion, Marissa tries to learn the truth. As Peter comes clean, she must decide how to protect the family

programme.title: Euphoria
programme.desc: Sinking into debt, Nate tries to partner with other investors, while Rue is recruited to work as a manager at one of Alamos strip clubs

programme.title: The Last of Us
programme.desc: As a storm brews in the mountains, the residents of Jackson Hole prepare for the worst amid increased sightings of infected. Meanwhile, Abby weighs up her options

programme.desc: Part three of three. Pembleton extracts a confession from the suspect in the shootings - who is later found with a bullet in his own head. Gritty police drama, with Andre Braugher

programme.desc: Bayliss goes it alone to investigate the murder of a cop shooter when his colleagues refuse to help him. Gritty police drama, starring Daniel Baldwin and Kyle Secor

programme.title: Homicide: Life on the Street
programme.desc: Howard returns to work, still feeling weak after the shooting, but solves a murder case from her desk to prove her mettle. Gritty police drama, starring Melissa Leo

programme.title: Urban Secrets
programme.desc: Alan Cumming heads across the Irish Sea to Dublin, where he succumbs to the citys famous charm and meets some of its most fascinating characters. On a journey that takes in an 18th-century pleasure house and a burlesque life-painting class, Alan also stops off in Temple Bar for a pint with singer Sharon Corr, unlocks a mystery in Irelands oldest library and uncovers a stylish secret that lurks behind the steel shutters of an old lock-up

programme.title: Urban Secrets
programme.desc: Alan Cumming visits Glasgow, one of his favourite cities, to reveal unusual and often hidden sights and sounds overlooked by the public. Starting at the Necropolis, he travels to the worlds oldest music hall before enjoying the views from a city-centre landmark. He also finds the time to take tea with a film star

programme.title: Fish Town
programme.desc: Life in the Devon fishing port of Brixham. Spring is in the air, and the town is a hive of activity as skippers return their pleasure yachts to the water after winter in the dry dock, while the pressure is on for Brixham to look its best for the arrival of Princess Anne, who will officially open the new £20million fish market, and the start of the summer season

programme.title: Fish Town
programme.desc: Brixham hosts its annual Pirate Festival, paying tribute to Devons colourful maritime history, while fisherman Nathan sets off in search of a lucrative catch
